The ghoul is currently in shock, but it will soon recover and attack anyone it sees. It can be captured to a holding platform for further study.

Capture or kill it before it wakes.

{USER_labelShort} has gained {USER_possessive} first level of cryptid insight! {USER_pronoun} is now an occultist.
{USER_labelShort} must build up cryptid focus through meditation. The effectiveness of this meditation is increased if the person can focus on a specific object while meditating.

Different people are able to focus on different objects. This depends on the person's backstories, traits, and ranks. For example:

- Occultists with enclave ranks meditate on their assigned posts.
- Occultists with tribal childhoods meditate to cryptic shrines and totems.
- Ascetic occultists meditate to blank walls.
- Psychopathic occultists meditate to graves, ideally with a loved one inside.

You can see someone's meditation focus types by looking at their info card with the 'i' button.
{USER_labelShort} has gained a new level of cryptid insight! {USER_pronoun} can now use cryptic revelations of that higher level, but {USER_pronoun} needs to learn them first.
Upon gaining the new cryptid insight level, {USER_labelShort} automatically learned these revelations: {2}
Use cryptid essence to learn {0} revelation

Cryptid focus is a special type of mental structuring required to use cryptic revelations. Cryptid focus dissipates over time and must be built by regular meditation. Losing all cryptid focus makes someone unable to use revelations.\n\nHigher cryptid focus permits higher-level revelations, but also increases the rate of focus dissipation.
Cryptid focus falls at {0} per day.

The lander's drive sputters dangerously as it nears your colony. It is going to crash.

Your short-range radio has picked up an automated distress call from a crippled vessel drifting in-system. As you watch its passage, a battered emergency lander breaks away from the hull and fires its drive for a last descent. Soon after, you receive a transmission from the passengers: they picked up your flight beacon.

The pilot explains that the craft is too unstable to perform a controlled descent and begs you to clear them for an emergency landing. He offers some advanced equipment as a reward.

You can accept their request, but be warned: the lander's damaged reactor could detonate during atmospheric entry, raining debris down on your colony. It might even explode on impact, wreaking havoc on your vertibird facilities.
The lander's drive holds steady as it approaches your beacon. It is going to make it.
The lander's reactor lost containment during atmospheric entry. Your flight beacon has picked up {0} inbound chunks of shrapnel and debris.
It seems the vertibird contains a small assortment of useful things.
It seems the vertibird contains a large assortment of useful things.
It seems the vertibird is empty. Its engines are damaged, making it useful only for scrap.
The vertibird's engine spins up as you approach, and it takes off for parts unknown.
The vertibird depressurizes violently when you approach, hurling several desiccated skeletons out of its cabin.
A piercing mental scream emits from the vertibird as you approach. A psychic charge.
The vertibird's hatch springs open when you approach, and a small attack party from {0} pours out.
The vertibird's reactor begins to wail louder and louder as you approach. It is a bomb.

Your Brotherhood flight beacon has detected an unidentified vertibird approaching on autopilot. Its transponder has been disabled, and all attempts to contact any crew onboard have failed.

However, analysis of its trajectory suggests it carries a heavy payload. It could be something valuable... or it could be a trap.

You can quash your beacon to hide from the approach, or keep it active and let the vertibird land.
A vertibird is landing to pick up {0}.
Clear the vertibird for emergency landing.

Your short-range radio crackles to life with a distressed Brotherhood pilot from {0}. Their medevac vertibird is carrying a badly injured ally.

Unfortunately, the patient’s condition has deteriorated rapidly, and the onboard medic fears they may not reach the nearest {0} base with proper medical facilities in time. Your settlement, however, is only a few minutes away.

The pilot and medic both implore you to clear them for an emergency landing so you can stabilize the patient. They cannot promise a concrete material reward, but they swear they will put in a good word with {1} and try to route some token of gratitude your way.
Some time ago, you assisted a distressed Brotherhood medevac vertibird. The pilot and medic kept their word and managed to have a reward drop sent your way.

You've just received a proposition from {0}. They need professional help with a particularly complex and risky {1} job, and they think one of your colonists - {2} - is exactly who they need.

If you accept, {0} will send a vertibird to ferry {2} to the worksite, where {3} will {4} The job will take 10 to 15 days, after which {2} will be flown back on a second vertibird alongside the promised reward.

However, {0} warns that while they can say {2} is not at risk of death, they cannot guarantee {2}'s general safety and health while on the job.

If you choose to accept, {0} offers the following reward: {5}

Flush any remaining structural heat from the Hellion reactor onto this gravheat absorber, allowing for an immediate Hellion launch. The gravheat absorber will enter a long cooldown period during which it cannot absorb any more heat.

Your Hellion has launched. You can now decide whether you want to keep the previous map around, or if you want to discard it.

If you keep it around, it will still be present as a colony map until you abandon it.

If you discard it, it may be replaced by an abandoned settlement, returned to its original owner, turned back into a special or quest location, or destroyed.
Please note that keeping several maps open may affect your performance.

Your Hellion has launched. You can now decide whether you want to settle the previous map.

If you settle it, it will still be present as a colony map until you abandon it.

If you decide not to settle it, the map will still be kept around until it is discarded automatically. It is currently kept around due to you having colonists there, a shuttle or drop pods travelling there, or some special building present on the map.

Among the remnants of the crashed robot platform, a still-intact Hellion reactor hums with energy.

You can build Hellion deck plating around the reactor to create a new aircraft. The assembly sustains the Hellion's lift envelope and allows long-distance travel.

Launching the Hellion will abandon your current map unless you build a mooring mast first.

You've discovered the location of an asteroid orbiting the planet. It contains significant amounts of {RESOURCE}, and potentially other resources.

Orbital miners steer valuable asteroids into planetary orbit so that they can be easily mined and sold to orbital traders. You do not detect any signs of life from this asteroid.

You can reach the asteroid with cargo bots, vertibirds, or the Hellion.

An ancient reactor is hidden within nearby ruins. The reactor is damaged and leaking, covering the region in toxic fallout. Locate and disable the reactor to obtain a reactor core and stop the fallout.
The malfunctioning terraformer has encased itself in solid ice. The reactor-core signal is coming from somewhere within. Find and destroy the terraformer to obtain the reactor core.

The reactor-core signal is coming from one of the nearby underground stockpiles. Hack the entrances and search the stockpiles to find the reactor core.\n\nBe careful, some of the defense systems may still be active.
This orbital platform is built to service and deploy combat robots, likely as part of some ancient planet-spanning war machine.\n\nThe reactor-core signal is coming from somewhere within.
The people who once inhabited this orbital platform are long gone and its original purpose has long been forgotten.\n\nThe reactor-core signal is coming from somewhere within.
The remains of an orbital platform are strewn nearby. Be careful, the wreckage contains hostile robots.\n\nThe reactor-core signal is coming from somewhere within the crash site.

The robot command hub's scanning process is close to determining your location. When it locates the Hellion, it will send a massive robot force to kill you.\n\nGather your supplies, then flee the map by selecting the cockpit console and selecting launch.

You have detected a massive Hellion-systems energy reading indicating that the robot command hub is hidden somewhere in orbit. If you could find it, you could shut it down - or make use of its power.

Robot command hubs are production-and-control platforms that manufacture robots and coordinate their actions around the planet. They are powered by a persona core.

Collect more Hellion components to track down the robot command hub's location.

{PAWN_labelShort} has equipped a RobCo Pipboy! {PAWN_pronoun} is now a RobCo mechanic and can create and control RobCo bots.

Research RobCo technology to get started.

(*SectionTitle)Creating bots:(/SectionTitle) Your mechanic can create bots at a RobCo workbench.

(*SectionTitle)About bandwidth:(/SectionTitle) Mechanitors can control as many bots as their total bandwidth allows. Select {PAWN_labelShort} and look at {PAWN_possessive} 'Bandwidth' readout to see how much bandwidth {PAWN_pronoun} is currently using and how much {PAWN_pronoun} has available. Bandwidth can be increased with certain apparel and by building RobCo band node buildings.

(*SectionTitle)About control groups:(/SectionTitle) Mechanics control bots in groups. Each control group can be set to one of these modes:

- Work
- Escort
- Recharge
- Dormant self-charge

To learn more about what each mode does, select the work mode icon on one of {PAWN_labelShort}'s control groups and hover over the different work orders. The maximum number of control groups can be increased with apparel and holotapes.

(*SectionTitle)About threat calling:(/SectionTitle) Mechanics can signal ancient leader bots to attack in order to obtain rare items from them. You must do this to improve your mechanitor past certain limits, but beware the danger.

{0} has encountered a Whitespring patrol from {1}. They have been spotted. {0} can try to escape, but this will inevitably increase the visibility of {2}. Fighting the patrol may be dangerous, but it will keep {2} safe. The patrol consists of:\n\n{3}
{0} has surrendered {1} to the Whitespring Enclave. It is unlikely you will ever see them again. Whitespring command does not take kindly to saboteurs.

{0} has dispatched a number of Vertibots to your location. They suspect you are harboring Brotherhood expeditionaries with hostile intent toward the Whitespring Enclave.\nA Vertibot is an unmanned atmospheric weapons platform designed for military suppression. Controlled from secure Whitespring installations, it is a highly effective tool for eliminating enemies of the Enclave. Equipped with advanced sensors and targeting systems, a Vertibot can detect and track targets from long distances. Once a target has been identified, it unleashes a devastating salvo of precision rockets designed to cause maximum damage and destruction.\nThe Whitespring Enclave has pinpointed the location of your critical infrastructure and the Vertibots will shortly begin their bombardment. Evacuation is advised.

The orbital beam satellites have finished arranging themselves above your colony. What follows can only be described as total destruction. The beams carve across the ground, creating a web of energy that systematically levels everything in its path. Terrain buckles and heaves as the fire slices through the earth, leaving behind deep crevices and rubble-filled craters. People, vehicles, and buildings are reduced to dust and ash in seconds. The air itself becomes superheated, creating a blast wave that can travel for miles.\nThere is no escape from the orbital inferno. Those caught in its path are instantly vaporized, leaving behind only charred remnants of what once stood there. Even underground shelters are no match for the destructive power of the beams.\nThe aftermath of the orbital inferno is a wasteland of ruin. The landscape is unrecognizable, with little more than rubble and ash as far as the eye can see. The air is thick with the smell of burnt flesh and melting metal. The once-thriving colony is now nothing more than a scorched husk.
Due to Whitespring disinformation, your relations with {0} have dropped by 10.
Visibility will increase by {0}. This may lead to more and stronger Whitespring responses.
Visibility will decrease by {0}. This may reduce Whitespring responses.
Biosecured crates can only be opened by characters with a Whitespring official backstory.
Goodwill with {0} increased by 5 due to positive rumors from Brotherhood expeditionaries.
{0} from {1} are attempting to avenge the fallen Whitespring official {2}.
Your prewar tech shipment has arrived.
Building a burnout intel scraper will be considered a declaration of war against the Whitespring Enclave.
Enable the intel scraper countdown, which will release a pulse every 60 seconds. This gives you a chance to obtain valuable intel, but it may also trigger a Whitespring investigation.
Burnout intel scraper intercepted Whitespring communication and generated {0} intel.

Burnout intel scraper attracted Whitespring attention and increased your visibility by {0}. Current visibility is {1}.
Burnout intel scraper was intercepted by a Whitespring patrol nearby. They are moving in to engage.

Items purchased through the prewar tech network do not arrive in your colony by cargo pods. Instead, a dead drop pickup quest becomes available after some time, and it remains active only briefly. This is where you collect the purchased prewar tech.

Time to receive and time to pick up depend on the amount of items you are purchasing, as well as your current visibility level. After the receive time has elapsed, a dead drop quest becomes available. It remains active only for the duration shown in 'Time to pick up'. If you fail to collect it in time, the items are gone.
This is a Brotherhood expeditionary quest. It may provide intel on the Whitespring Enclave, but it may also increase your faction's visibility.
You can spend intel to obtain quests from the Brotherhood network. However, you will not know what rewards a quest offers until you purchase it.
Visibility gain from Enclave surveillance pillar: {0}

The Whitespring Enclave, once a symbol of secrecy, privilege, and oppression, has crumbled. All over the region, Brotherhood cells and allied defectors rose in rebellion, breaking the machinery that kept the Enclave in power. Every child who now wakes to the dawn of freedom will remember the names of the people who dared to challenge the impossible:
{0}

However, this is not the end, only a transition. With the old government in ruins and the Kovac-Muldoon Platform reduced to a burning wreck in the sky, the time for healing and rebuilding has begun. Former Whitespring officials now scatter, surrender, or vanish into the wasteland. There is no denying the truth: the world has changed forever.

As you stand at the threshold of that new era, you remember this chapter of defiance and sacrifice. The fall of the Whitespring Enclave will remain a testament to your resilience and to the Brotherhood's refusal to bow. You step forward into a future built on hard-won freedom, vigilance, and hope.

[PAWN_nameDef] became famous among engineers for designing a prototype fusion system intended to restore entire regional grids. Driven by a relentless need to solve catastrophic power shortages, [PAWN_pronoun] pushed dangerous research farther than anyone else would dare. Early reactor trials were rushed and unstable, and the accidents that followed cost many lives. Those failures never stopped [PAWN_objective]; they hardened [PAWN_possessive] belief that the work had to be finished. After countless revisions, [PAWN_nameDef] produced the prototype that made [PAWN_objective] a hero to some and a reckless visionary to others.

[PAWN_nameDef] coordinated Operation: Deadlife from a buried continuity bunker, convinced that a ruined world could still be ruled if the right people endured below. When the bombs fell, [PAWN_pronoun] sealed the command complex, rationed the survivors, and kept issuing orders long after the chain of command had died. Radiation, age, and emergency preservation treatments left [PAWN_objective] a thinking ghoul in dress uniform, half sustained by medicine and half by obsession. [PAWN_pronoun] still waits in the control room, ready to finish the final launch order.

A veteran Brotherhood vertibird pilot, [PAWN_nameDef] has hauled patrols, wounded, cargo, and dignitaries through weather and gunfire alike. [PAWN_pronoun] keeps a cool head, knows flightline discipline, and can trade stories with grunts and brass in equal measure.

[PAWN_nameDef] designed the construction of buildings. Back home, the technical calculations were handled by automated systems while the labour was done by work crews and machines. This freed [PAWN_objective] up to push the artistic limits of the craft, but also meant [PAWN_pronoun] never had to get [PAWN_possessive] hands dirty.

[PAWN_nameDef] managed [PAWN_possessive] family's investments in the arts.\n\n[PAWN_pronoun] chose artists to patronize and worked with them to produce the expressions that most glorified [PAWN_possessive] family, their church, and the ruling order itself.

[PAWN_nameDef] lived in a wealthy sealed resort where robots handled the menial work and the residents devoted themselves to leisure. [PAWN_pronoun] managed the plant and animal life of a sprawling park where guests came to admire nature's beauty.

Among tunnel-dwellers, those with vision as strong as [PAWN_nameDef]'s were revered as guides. [PAWN_pronoun] led the way through the dark, marking good digging spots with fungus lanterns and warning others of danger.
[PAWN_nameDef] grew up in a cave settlement deep beneath the surface of an inhospitable region. [PAWN_pronoun] worked with the other children tending the settlement's fungus crops.
[PAWN_nameDef] worked as a digger in a massive underground cave network.\n\n[PAWN_pronoun] knows rock so well that [PAWN_pronoun] can almost navigate caves by smell.
The richest old-world communities lived in comfort while nearby slums starved. [PAWN_nameDef] spent [PAWN_possessive] life appealing for donations and supporting those in need.

[PAWN_nameDef] was the star chef at a high-class resort restaurant. [PAWN_pronoun] was famous among patrons for [PAWN_possessive] creative culinary specialties, and infamous among the kitchen staff for [PAWN_possessive] casual disdain for grunt work.

[PAWN_nameDef] was recruited by the military as an engineer and set to work improving the army's vertibirds and armored carriers. The harsh training taught [PAWN_objective] how to build and repair military vehicles and structures.\n\nOne mission left [PAWN_nameDef] with pyrophobia and a strong desire to avoid plants.

[PAWN_nameDef] grew up in a privileged Enclave family estate in Appalachia. [PAWN_possessive] early years were filled with lessons in etiquette, politics, and Whitespring traditions.\n[PAWN_nameDef] and [PAWN_possessive] friends found ways to sneak out and explore the forests beyond the compound.

Growing up sheltered in a vast old-world arcology, [PAWN_nameDef] distracted [PAWN_objective]self by playing wargames and painting miniatures. While [PAWN_pronoun] was never very good at the game itself, it helped connect [PAWN_objective] with others.

[PAWN_nameDef] found it easy to relate to others and mediate conflict. [PAWN_pronoun] was selected for a government emotional training program. The training intensified [PAWN_possessive] natural abilities, but [PAWN_pronoun] became unable to cause others pain.

Though [PAWN_nameDef]'s posting was comfortable, [PAWN_possessive] unit stayed ready for war. [PAWN_nameDef] pursued artistic hobbies in [PAWN_possessive] off hours, but [PAWN_possessive] subordinates preferred to settle disputes with their fists, so [PAWN_nameDef] got good at breaking up fights.

[PAWN_nameDef] worked as a surgeon in a wealthy district mostly free of disease and hardship, so [PAWN_possessive] job focused on elaborate and creative cosmetic procedures.\n\n[PAWN_pronoun] understands human biology deeply, but has never had to remove a tumour or a bullet under battlefield conditions.

There is a law that says you are not allowed to bolt heavy plasma emitters onto a cargo hauler, especially if you hide them under what looks like a communications rig for a cleaner ambush. [PAWN_nameDef] discovered that there is great profit to be made from customers who do not care about these laws.

[PAWN_nameDef] worked as a military police officer within the Whitespring, hunting unorthodox thought and dissent.\n\n[PAWN_nameDef] exposed and punished deviance to maintain order and the enclave's research priorities.

[PAWN_nameDef] was a lawyer in the Whitespring division.\n\n[PAWN_pronoun] spent half [PAWN_possessive] time studying laws and the role of the order within enclave doctrine. The other half was spent preaching discipline and loyalty.

[PAWN_nameDef] worked in a city lab developing neural entertainment software to maximize user pleasure.\n\nWhen the local government imposed harsh restrictions on the product, [PAWN_pronoun] began selling [PAWN_possessive] designs on the black market.
[PAWN_nameDef] worked in a comfortable research district developing hyper-realistic war simulations.
Born and raised in a wealthy enclave district, [PAWN_nameDef] became obsessed with simulation software. [PAWN_pronoun] spent far too much time plugged in.

After being laid off by a giant old-world corporation, [PAWN_nameDef] spent several years disillusioned and fighting alcohol addiction.\n\n[PAWN_pronoun] ended up busking aboard a battered cargo hauler, playing a beaten-up guitar and entertaining the crew with blues and folk songs from the distant past.

Modern wars are won by technology, so old-world naval labs hoarded the best equipment they could find. [PAWN_nameDef] worked in one of those labs, studying captured prototypes and strange relics hauled in from distant expeditions.

[PAWN_nameDef] lived in a prosperous pre-war city, penning wildly successful novels. [PAWN_pronoun] lived a carefree lifestyle, spending [PAWN_possessive] days writing and [PAWN_possessive] nights partying, and has never done a day's manual labour in [PAWN_possessive] life.

[PAWN_nameDef] designed combat modifications for frontline soldiers.\n\nOne night, in a nightmare, [PAWN_nameDef] was confronted by all the men and women killed by [PAWN_possessive] work. [PAWN_pronoun] decided to head into the wastes and use [PAWN_possessive] skills for good.

[PAWN_nameDef] was born on the dark edge of a sprawling industrial city. After an argument, [PAWN_possessive] father sold [PAWN_objective] to a local boss as a slave artist.\n\nForced to create art or be punished, [PAWN_pronoun] withdrew and became unable to form relations with others.

[PAWN_nameDef] made meals for Whitespring patrons.\n\nOnly the finest food was acceptable, so [PAWN_nameDef] tended a small garden on the compound grounds to get the freshest herbs and vegetables. [PAWN_pronoun] fell in love with the food [PAWN_objective]self.

[PAWN_nameDef] served wealthy Whitespring patrons as a massage artist. [PAWN_pronoun] serviced many members of the enclave and their guests.\n\n[PAWN_nameDef] occasionally offered something extra, if the patron was in the mood. Advancing in this line of work meant having refined social skills.

Born to the administrators of a remote government outpost, [PAWN_nameDef] was enrolled in a youth program that taught military scouting skills.\n\n[PAWN_pronoun] learned to survive in the wilderness, to obey, and not to ask questions.

[PAWN_nameDef] started off sculpting person-sized artworks, but [PAWN_pronoun] soon began to think bigger. With the help of industrial equipment, [PAWN_pronoun] learned how to carve huge monuments out of cliffsides. Soon the parks and promenades of the city where [PAWN_pronoun] lived were full of giant stone figures.

[PAWN_nameDef]'s family held their power base in a grand, ancient command bunker. Born and raised inside it, [PAWN_nameDef] learned all about sealed-facility life, as well as the politics and methods of siege warfare and bunker assaults.\n\n[PAWN_pronoun] learned to feel most at home with strong walls close on all sides and the gentle hiss of air scrubbers.

[PAWN_nameDef] was born to low-ranked servants in an ancient command bunker. The bunker mainframe was like a third parent to [PAWN_objective], and the maintenance crew [PAWN_pronoun] worked for was like a second family.\n\n[PAWN_nameDef] rarely saw plants or animals, and still finds them unnerving to touch.

[PAWN_nameDef]'s role was to blast into fortified bunkers, punch through the interior defenses, and conquer them in room-to-room combat. [PAWN_pronoun] became very good at wielding heavy breaching weapons to cut both steel and flesh.\n\n[PAWN_pronoun] also developed a taste for the drugs [PAWN_pronoun] used to cope with the overwhelming stress.

[PAWN_nameDef] was raised in the ancient command bunker where [PAWN_possessive] parents were stationed. [PAWN_pronoun] learned the basics of firearm operations and maintenance from the soldiers on duty and became accustomed to a strict routine and frequent discipline.

[PAWN_nameDef] was a shock trooper in an elite army. [PAWN_possessive] job was to blast through fortified positions, gun down the defenders, and capture the place intact. And [PAWN_pronoun] was good at it.

[PAWN_nameDef] finally managed to flee [PAWN_possessive] criminal life by getting a maintenance job at a nearby relay station. [PAWN_pronoun] performed a wide range of repair and construction work, helped with basic research, and often defused co-workers' arguments. [PAWN_pronoun] never liked doing art.

While other residents slept through long stretches in a sealed government bunker, [PAWN_nameDef] had to wake up at periodic intervals to perform inspections, check the life-support systems, and oil the maintenance robots.
[PAWN_nameDef] traveled from region to region, studying and classifying the plant and animal life [PAWN_pronoun] found in the hope of creating a complete catalogue.

In the most corrupt old cities, scientists without a moral compass committed unspeakable atrocities in the name of research. [PAWN_nameDef] was kept alone in a sealed facility from birth and subjected to behavioural experiments in an attempt to turn [PAWN_objective] into a perfect super-soldier.

[PAWN_nameDef] worked as a mindless construction hand in a massive company town. Though [PAWN_pronoun] is now free from the brutal social conditioning [PAWN_pronoun] was subjected to, [PAWN_pronoun] remains incapable of creative thought and leadership.

In the old corporate cities, most people suffered. But someone had to run the companies.\n\n[PAWN_nameDef] learned the skills of the trade, greasing palms and balancing technical analysis with politics. [PAWN_pronoun] became a sociointellectual machine.

It is no secret that the companies own much of the old cities, but someone has to take the bribes. [PAWN_nameDef] was one such figurehead.\n\n[PAWN_pronoun] learned how to manipulate the hopes and dreams of the people while living comfortably in a penthouse.
Most people around [PAWN_nameDef] lived under constant corporate surveillance and propaganda feeds. [PAWN_nameDef] joined a rebel faction that rejected modern technology entirely.

The old industrial cities were ancient, deep warrens of rust, poison, and too many people. [PAWN_nameDef] grew up in the dark, unwanted reaches of one of them and had to fight for every scrap of food.

[PAWN_nameDef] designed virtual reality simulations for wealthy patrons. [PAWN_pronoun] put great effort into the details of [PAWN_possessive] virtual worlds, learning all [PAWN_pronoun] could about plant life to make [PAWN_possessive] landscapes as realistic as possible.
[PAWN_nameDef] designed elaborate entertainment mazes for wealthy patrons. Even now, [PAWN_pronoun] thinks of little else.

[PAWN_nameDef] grew up in a research camp on a blasted world. [PAWN_possessive] parents were studying mysterious labyrinthine formations beneath the crust, and [PAWN_pronoun] spent most of [PAWN_possessive] time wandering those tunnels and wondering what kind of thing could have created them.
Concrete channels and cracked embankments still drag water across the heartland. Where the old system holds, the land is greener, richer, and fought over all the harder.
Oak and pine woodland covers the rolling middle country between valley and mountain. It is gentler than the wastes below, though raiders, predators, and hungry brahmin still leave their mark.
Where water cuts into the desert, steep walls, dry shelves, and stubborn life gather around the channel. These canyons offer shade and ambush in equal measure.
These cold creek bottoms cut through the preserve with soft ground, brush, and black water. They are rich compared to the uplands, but travel is slow and the wet gets everywhere.
Wide interior flats once fed cities from Sacramento to Bakersfield. Now the valley is still fertile by wasteland standards, but dust, heat, and ruin press in from every side.
Tangled wet ground, red growth, and strange light make these bogs feel wrong even before night falls. They are rich, eerie places where beauty and decay have learned to share the same water.
The bomb fell hardest here. Glassed earth, shattered concrete, and poisoned dust fill the impact zones where cities and test sites were erased in an instant.
The land here is dry, broken, and bitter, split by ridges, gullies, and old fire scars. Water is scarce, cover is thin, and every mile feels farther than it should.
Broad rivers spill out over flat country, leaving behind mud, reeds, and good soil. These bottoms can be abundant in the right season and miserable in the wrong one.
These frozen flats thaw only a little before winter takes them again. Sparse brush and wandering herds can survive here, but the cold rules everything.
Ancient ice lies over the land in hard blue-white plates. There is no soil, almost no life, and only the desperate or the doomed try to make a home here.
Black volcanic stone and raw earth make this country look half-finished by the world. Heat lingers in the rock, plant life is thin, and the ground feels hostile even when it stands still.
Along the coast, salt wind blows through rotting piers, stranded hulls, and drowned suburbs. The sea softens the temperature here, but not the dangers that washed in with the old world.
High desert plateaus sit above the low country in wind and heat. The ground is harsh, the horizons are wide, and survival depends on seeing trouble long before it reaches you.
The open desert bakes under a white sun and cools fast after dark. Scrub, stone, and long empty distances define this country, along with the bones of highways that once crossed it.
Cold evergreen forest and marshy lowland meet in these protected backwoods. The air is clean, the game is wary, and old-world quiet still survives between the trees.
Great red trunks rise over cool mountain fog and dim forest ground. These woods feel older than the war, and deep enough to hide both safe camps and things better left alone.
Beyond the ground zeroes lie collapsed districts, dead roads, and the shells of neighborhoods that never recovered. There is scrap here, shelter too, and plenty willing to kill for both.
Broken white sheets drift over the northern sea, creaking under the wind and cold. Nothing grows here, little hunts here, and anyone caught out on the ice is far from help.
Warm mountain slopes carry giant trees, dry needles, and long views over the wastes below. The forest is beautiful from a distance and unforgiving once you are inside it.
Open plains of frost-hardened soil stretch beneath long gray skies. There are a few shrubs, a few animals, and just enough life to tempt settlers into staying.
These outer blasted belts were spared the worst of the fire and caught the long death after. Ash, rubble, and tainted soil stretch between civilization's corpse and the living frontier.
Culture originating from the pre-war United States.

Corvega factory systems for automating extraction, transfer, and fabrication across a proper pre-War works floor.
A divergent insect geneline adapted into aggressive fire ants. They share the same eusocial behavior as the black ant strain, but operate as a separate colony and field only ember-colored castes.
Descendants of vault dwellers or surface survivors who abandoned technology generations ago. They worship spirits of the old world and wield makeshift weapons crafted from scrap and bone.

The Civil Arizona Settlement is a faction of survivors and settlers who have banded together to rebuild society in the aftermath of a devastating conflict. They are known for their resourcefulness, resilience, and commitment to community values. The CCS emphasizescooperation and mutual aid among its members, striving to create a safe and prosperous environment despite the challenges they face in the post-apocalyptic world.

The Arizona Raider Gang thrives on chaos and plunder. Forged in the ashes of civilization, they scavenge, raid, and rule through fear. Their members are scarred, loud, and proud of their savagery, painting their armor with the symbols of past kills. They move in convoys of patched-together vehicles, hitting trade routes and settlements for supplies, slaves, or sport. To them, the wasteland isnâ€™t a tragedy â€” itâ€™s freedom without rules.

The Rough Arizona Settlement is a loose collection of survivors hardened by the wastelandâ€™s brutality. They value grit over grace, and survival over civility. Life here is harsh â€” trade is bartered through intimidation, disputes are settled with fists or gunfire, and law depends on whoâ€™s standing last. Despite their rough edges, theyâ€™ve carved out a functioning, if volatile, society where toughness earns respect and weakness finds no quarter.

The Arizona Tribe traces its roots to survivors who turned back to the old ways when the world fell apart. They live close to the land, guided by oral tradition, ancestor worship, and a deep respect for the cycles of nature. Though primitive by modern standards, they are skilled hunters, gatherers, and healers who blend salvaged pre-war tools with tribal craftsmanship. They defend their territory fiercely but trade fairly with outsiders â€” if they prove worthy of trust.
