Ruthless scavengers and murderers who live for the thrill of domination. They often mark their armor with blood and chems, worshiping violence as the only truth.

Ruthless and unyielding, the Antagonizer is a force of destruction and discord. Their motives shrouded in darkness, they seek power through chaos, crushing all opposition without mercy. Cross their path, and youâ€™ll know true enmity in the wasteland.

Flesh freed from chains, the Escaped Slaves bear scars both seen and unseen. Bonded by suffering and driven by hope, they struggle for freedom and justice amidst the wastelandâ€™s cruelty. Fierce and desperate, they fight for a life stolen but not forgotten.

Guardians of ancient wisdom, the Wise Mothman Cultists follow the elusive protector as a guide against the wastelandâ€™s creeping horrors. Calm and watchful, they seek balance between man and myth, standing sentinel against the chaos that threatens to consume all.
A new settlement of various outcasts and wanderers.

The Enclave Radicals burn with fanatic fire, wielding doctrine like a blade. Purity, dominance, and absolute control drive them to purge what they deem impure. Their zeal knows no bounds, turning even allies into enemies in their relentless crusade.

In the frozen wilds, the Wendigo Cultists whisper dark rites to the ancient spirit of hunger and death. Their bones chilled by bitter winds, they embrace the curse of the Wendigo with fevered devotion. Beware their wild eyes â€” they hunger not just for flesh, but for souls.

Scattered and hunted, the Enclave Remnants cling to the last fragments of a fallen empire. Proud and unbroken, they uphold their sacred mission: to reclaim humanity through strength and technology. Their shadows stretch long, shaped by secrets and betrayal.
The crew assigned to the Hellion.

Crafted from the remnants of a lost age, the Mechanist commands legions of cold steel and gears. With an unyielding devotion to technology, they seek to build a new order where machines reign supreme, and flesh is secondary. Their creations march endlessly â€” silent, relentless, perfect.

These people have lived here for decades or centuries and lost most of the technology that first brought them to this world. They can still defend themselves with basic gunpowder weapons while going about their daily routine of herding cattle, drinking rot gut, and hunting down bounties.

They care about trade, trust, and survival.

This particular group holds civil behavior in high regard.

These people have lived here for decades or centuries and lost most of the technology that first brought them to this world. They can still defend themselves with basic gunpowder weapons while going about their daily routine of herding cattle, drinking rot gut, and hunting down bounties.

They are not concerned with trade or trust.

This particular group believes that no one is created equal, and only strength allows one to survive on the frontier.

These post-war traders are not a feudal guild but a loose network of caravan companies, brahmin drovers, guards, and deal-brokers who keep the wasteland moving. They abandon static lordships in favor of mobile camps, barter routes, and temporary markets, hauling scarce goods between isolated communities while armed escorts keep raiders honest.
This Settlers scenario has been disabled by Sunset.
A militant enclave of survivors claiming to represent the Appalachia scientific division. They pursue research into cryptids and hoard intelligence and technology for the Whitespring Enclave.
A Brotherhood expeditionary cell, laying low.
An organized Brotherhood expeditionary force dedicated to disrupting the Whitespring Enclave.

Heat trapped beneath a filthy radioactive haze has driven local temperatures to punishing levels.

Flora and fauna are suffering as biomes drift toward their hotter counterparts. Water dries up, forage fails, and every faction in the wasteland feels the pressure.

It can last anywhere between a season and several years.

High-altitude ash and radioactive soot have choked the sky, plunging the region into a nuclear winter.

Flora and fauna are suffering as biomes slide toward their colder counterparts. Food and fuel become scarce, and wasteland factions grow more desperate by the day.

It can last anywhere between a season and several years.

Orbital debris and black ash have smothered the sun, throwing the region into a long, ruinous twilight.

With the sky darkened and visibility ruined, wasteland factions take advantage of the confusion to raid, move, and settle scores under cover of gloom.

It can last anywhere between a season and several years.

A surge of unstable spores and irradiated pollen has reshaped local plant life almost overnight.

The sky glows with strange color while mutant flowers spread through fields, roads, and ruins alike. Colonists feel oddly uplifted, but the overgrowth can smother your crops if left unchecked.

It can last anywhere between a few days and a season.

An unstable old-world weather anomaly has churned a temporal radstorm into the atmosphere.

The storm lashes the land with strange glowing rain that twists flesh and time alike. Anyone left in the open will age at a frightening pace.

It can last anywhere between a season and several years.
A running vertibird dogfight is raging overhead. Wreckage and missed shots can rain down across the area for several hours.

Detonation of Deadlife dust warheads in the upper atmosphere has permanently altered planetary conditions. A dense death pall now blankets the world. This pall reanimates unburied corpses exposed to the open air as feral ghouls.

The Whitespring Enclave has initiated an orbital inferno protocol on your colony and is moving the required orbital beam satellites into position. Once they align overhead, they will glass the colony with numerous pillars of fire. This can be prevented by reducing your visibility back to Hidden.
A Vertibot strike is underway. Take cover!
A hardy caste of treeminder, the radbarkling is covered in thick, bark-like hide infused with radiation. It excels at absorbing damage in combat, protecting its tree and allies.
A dryad caste specialized in food production, the mutfruitling generates nourishing mutfruit on an ongoing basis. However, it is slow and ineffective at work or combat.
A helpful caste of treeminder, the portling is specialized in carrying items. Under human influence, it hauls goods where they need to go, but is slow and ineffective at work or combat.
A fierce caste of treeminder, the scratchling is bred for aggression and damage-dealing attacks. Its oversized claws can tear through most threats, but it is vulnerable to retaliation.
A unique caste of treeminder, the sproutling can produce new Gauranlen pods and help reproduce the treefather. Three sproutlings must slowly merge to complete the process.
A gentle caste of treeminder, the brocflowerling specializes in producing medicinal herbs. It works with its tree to generate healing plants, but is slow and ineffective at work or combat.

A slow, bumbling caste of treeminder which specializes in pungs production. Together with its Gauranlen tree, this dryad can generate punga fruit on an ongoing basis. However, it is slow and ineffective at work or combat.

A treeminder caste specialized in producing a shaggy, leather-like hide. Together with its Treefather, it can be sheared for a soft, insulative material on an ongoing basis, though it is slow and ineffective at work or combat.

A curious treeminder caste specialized in producing invigorating sap-fruit. Together with its Treefather, it can generate this stimulant crop on an ongoing basis, but it is completely defenseless and requires careful protection.
A treeminder caste specialized in territorial defense that can launch corrosive projectiles. Though it can dish out tremendous damage, it remains vulnerable in melee due to its oversized maw.
An extremely easy-going treeminder caste that specializes in dragging up stone chunks. It absorbs minerals from the ground to harden its hide while leaving the oversized pieces aside.

A slow, bumbling caste of treeminder, the scrapwoodling specializes in producing scrap wood. Together with its Gauranlen tree, it generates wood-like material on an ongoing basis, but is slow and ineffective at work or combat.

Carriers of this gene suffer from Wendigo Sickness, often called "The Shakes" or "Wendigo Shakes"â€”a vitamin deficiency caused by exclusive cannibalism. This condition causes the gums to withdraw, leaving the appearance of long, fang-like teeth. Afflicted individuals frequently experience catatonic muscle shakes, giving the syndrome its name.
Carriers can still draw nourishment from cold, spoiled blood. Wendigo survivors learn to bleed ruined corpses and irradiated remains when nothing living can sate the hunger.
Carriers burn through hemogen at a grotesque rate. Their starving, mutation-wracked metabolism strips nutrients faster than ordinary feeding can replace them.
Carriers of this gene stumble and trip, moving slowly and awkwardly through the ruins. Their lack of coordination makes escape and pursuit a challenge.
Carriers of this gene move at a standard pace, able to keep up with the group but rarely outpace danger. Their reflexes are reliable, if not remarkable.
Carriers of this gene are nimble and quick, darting through the wasteland with impressive speed. Their reflexes and coordination are a cut above the rest.
Carriers of this gene are legends of movement, outrunning threats and navigating obstacles with acrobatic grace. Their speed is matched only by their finesse.
Carriers of this gene repel others with awkwardness and rough manners, struggling to make friends or influence anyone. Their words fall flat, and their looks do little to help.
Carriers of this gene blend into the crowd, able to hold a conversation and get by, but rarely leaving a lasting impression. Their beauty and speech are unremarkable.
Carriers of this gene charm and persuade with ease, their words and looks opening doors and winning allies. They are the face of any group, and their presence is felt by all.
Carriers of this gene are wasteland celebrities, captivating crowds and swaying opinions with a glance or a word. Their beauty and eloquence are the stuff of legend.
Carriers of this gene are fragile, succumbing quickly to wounds, toxins, and pain. The harsh realities of the wasteland take their toll, and survival is a daily struggle.
Carriers of this gene endure the wastelandÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s hardships as well as most, surviving wounds and illness with no special advantage or disadvantage. Their bodies are steady, if not exceptional.
Carriers of this gene shrug off pain, toxins, and blood loss, surviving injuries that would fell lesser folk. Their stamina and resistance are a testament to their hardy lineage.
Carriers of this gene are nearly unstoppable, enduring wounds, poisons, and agony with legendary grit. Their bodies seem built for survival, no matter the odds.
The Atlantic embody adaptability Ã¢â‚¬â€ forged by ocean winds and shifting tides. Their blood sings with salt and stubborn endurance.
Children of misty forests and basalt mountains. The Cascadians are unshaken by rain or solitude, ever patient and feral at heart.
The Ninety-Nine Tribes carry ancestral echoes Ã¢â‚¬â€ every heart a drumbeat in a chorus of shared memory.
Dust storms and refinery fumes forged the Texicans Ã¢â‚¬â€ weathered souls fueled by stubborn pride and old oil.
The Numen are entwined with the land Ã¢â‚¬â€ their spirits a living testament to survival against all odds.
The Pure Humans embody the resilience of unaltered humanity Ã¢â‚¬â€ a steadfast spirit that endures through every trial.
The Slanters drift between worlds Ã¢â‚¬â€ dreamers and schemers with eyes like broken glass, caught between memory and myth.
The Super MutantsÃ¢â‚¬â„¢ strength is legend Ã¢â‚¬â€ a fusion of pain and pride that outlived their makers.
Split between paradise and ruin, the Two Californians live in contradiction Ã¢â‚¬â€ both dreamers and survivors of their own myth.
Raised under steel and silence, the Vault DwellersÃ¢â‚¬â„¢ heritage is order Ã¢â‚¬â€ a longing for structure even in chaos.
The Wendigo embody hunger made flesh Ã¢â‚¬â€ a spirit of endless wanting that no feast can sate.
Carriers of this gene struggle with even simple concepts, learning slowly and teaching little. The mysteries of technology and science remain forever out of reach.
Carriers of this gene grasp new ideas at a normal pace, able to learn and teach as well as most. Their minds are steady, if not brilliant.
Carriers of this gene are quick thinkers, mastering skills and sharing knowledge with ease. Their intellect is a valuable asset in the wastes.
Carriers of this gene are wasteland prodigies, solving problems and teaching others with genius-level insight. Their minds shape the future of civilization.
Carriers of this gene seem cursed, suffering frequent illness and slow recovery from wounds. Fortune rarely favors them, and danger is a constant companion.
Carriers of this gene experience the usual ups and downs of wasteland life, neither blessed nor cursed. Their health and fortune are as unpredictable as the weather.
Carriers of this gene are favored by fate, dodging disease and healing quickly from injuries. Good things seem to happen to them more often than not.
Carriers of this gene are wasteland legends, immune to misfortune and blessed with miraculous recoveries. Their luck is the envy of all who know them.
Carriers of this gene stumble through the wastes, missing every subtle sound and shadow. Their senses are dulled, making ambushes and hidden dangers a constant threat.
Carriers of this gene see and hear as well as the next vault dweller, catching most details but sometimes missing the finer points. Their awareness is reliable, if unremarkable.
Carriers of this gene have eyes like a hawk and ears tuned to the faintest whisper. They spot threats and opportunities before others, making them invaluable scouts and sharpshooters.
Carriers of this gene are the wastelandÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s living sensors, detecting danger and secrets with supernatural clarity. No ambush or hidden cache escapes their notice.

Carriers of this gene are the definition of frail, struggling to lift even the lightest gear and finding meals a challenge to chew and digest. Their bodies tire quickly, and every burden feels heavier than it should.
Carriers of this gene possess the strength of the everyday wastelander, able to carry their own and survive on standard rations. They neither excel nor falter in feats of muscle or digestion.
Carriers of this gene are built for the wastes, hauling heavy loads with ease and devouring even the toughest meals. Their bodies are resilient, and their stamina rarely fails them.

Carriers of this gene are legends among survivors, able to lift, carry, and consume with unmatched power. Their strength is the stuff of pre-war myths, and nothing seems too heavy or too tough for them.
A chemical addiction to punga fruit. Long-term use has caused neurological changes, making it hard to function without the fruit. Withdrawal is tough, but with time, the body can recover.
Punga chemicals in the bloodstream. It lifts the mood and helps survivors forget the harshness of the wasteland.
A built-up tolerance to punga fruit. The more severe this tolerance is, the more punga it takes to get the same effect.
This colonist drank an atomic cocktail that had pungashine as one of its ingredients.
Consumed a nice atomic cocktail. Has a chance to provide a random inspiration.
A burst of sugar from a bottle of homebrew sarsaparilla provides a short-lived lift. Provides a 5% manipulation buff for 5 hours.
An unpleasant delayed after-effect of guarana consumption.
Guarana restores rest immediately and increases movement speed, but it always leaves a crash behind it.
A dose of aqua cura is active in this person's bloodstream. If they die while it remains active, the suspended repair nanites will try to rebuild them on the spot.
A tangy cranberry sauce added depth and sweetness to this meal.
Intense nausea caused by sudden acceleration or violent deceleration aboard the Hellion.

A Pipboy holotape which increases a mechanic's control group count and the work speed of a mechanic's robots. This advanced sublink is encoded with proprietary RobCo firmware for optimal control and reliability. Can be installed up to 6 times for maximum effect.

A Pipboy holotape which accelerates robot crafting in the gestator. RobCo's proprietary processor firmware ensures maximum efficiency and reliability. Can be installed up to 6 times for increased effect.
A Pipboy holotape which allows remote repair of robots. Proprietary RobCo firmware enables remote fault restoration. Can be installed up to 3 times for increased range.

A Pipboy holotape which allows remote projection of personal defense shields onto robots. Proprietary RobCo firmware ensures reliable shield deployment. Can be installed up to 3 times for increased range and energy.

A Pipboy holotape which increases the speed of robot repairs. Direct neural interface and proprietary RobCo diagnostics allow rapid and reliable restoration. Can be installed up to 6 times for increased effect.
Receives a combat uplink from a nearby War Machine.
Severe exhaustion caused by prolonged Wendigo Shakes.
Metabolic activity is temporarily increased to fight the Wendigo Shakes.
Blood production is temporarily boosted to counteract the effects of the Wendigo Shakes.
Recovery from the Wendigo Shakes was interrupted, worsening symptoms.
Mental stability is temporarily increased to resist the Wendigo Shakes.
A stealthboy cloak granted by the Stealthboy device. It affects nearby observers, making their brains unable to perceive this individual even if their eyes remain unaffected.
Feral people live in harmony with the Treefather, relying on hunting and gathering for survival in the wasteland.
Followers revere the Treefather, a mutated tree that produces treeminders and pods, and maintain a sacred bond with nature.
The lights above and the ruined world below both speak in signs. We read the sky for warnings, promises, and the fate waiting out in the wastes.
No day is better spent than one in a hot forge. With hammers, kilns, and scrap steel, we turn hidden workshops into the backbone of a hard underground life.
We believe the old world still holds lessons worth reviving. Discipline, ritual, and a sharpened blade keep a people alive when former brothers turn on each other.
The iron rule of our supreme leader should never come into question. If those who oppose us will not kneel, we will break them until they do.

Our forebears survived by following warlords, bosses, and local strongmen, and we see no reason to change now. If neighboring settlements prefer blades and tribute, then tribute is what they will pay.
Through winter storms and broken coastlines, our shield-mothers lead the people. Die with honor or answer to the women who keep the warbands together.
draw rot gut from rot gut barrels
Stay near the mechanic and fight nearby enemies. Follow the mechanic, even off the map.

Certain ideologies promote the censorship and destruction of knowledge deemed dangerous or subversive. Book burning is a symbolic act representing the rejection of ideas that challenge the established order or threaten societal stability. This practice reflects the broader struggle for control over information and the shaping of cultural narratives in a post-apocalyptic world.

Brahmin Barons are individuals or groups who have mastered the art of breeding and raising Brahmin for profit. They control large herds, supplying meat, milk, and other products to settlements and traders across the region. Their influence often extends into local politics and economies, making them powerful figures in the post-apocalyptic society.

In the harsh world of Greenway, some families have turned to cannibalism as a means of survival. These families often operate in secret, preying on outsiders or even resorting to ritualistic practices. While viewed with horror by most, cannibalist families see their actions as a necessary adaptation to the brutal realities of their environment.

The trade of chems is a significant aspect of the local economy. Various factions and individuals engage in the production, distribution, and sale of chems, often leading to conflicts and power struggles. The popularity of chems among the populace creates a lucrative market, but also contributes to social issues such as addiction and crime.

Those who live in extreme texic entiroments are often weary of consuming traditional food sources, turning instead to food derived fromunderground sources, where they spend most of their time to avoid the toxic atmosphere.

The Desert Rangers are a respected group of abolitionist law enforcers, known for their strict adherence to justice and their ability to navigate the harsh desert environment. They serve as protectors of the people, maintaining order in a land rife with danger and lawlessness and liberating any slave they can.

Former scientists of the Enclave often find themselves in exile, working independently or with other factions. These scientists bring valuable knowledge and expertise, contributing to technological advancements and research efforts in the region. However, their association with the Enclave can also lead to mistrust and suspicion among other groups.

Followers of certain ideologies often provide mutual support and assistance to one another. This can include sharing resources, offering protection, and working together to achieve common goals. Such solidarity is crucial in the harsh environment of Greenway, where survival often depends on cooperation and trust among like-minded individuals.
A democratic government inspired by NCR and prewar city-states, with elected councils, traders, and wild west caravans.
A government led by mayors, kings, chiefs, or sheriffs, often corrupt or neutral peacekeepers, ruling by force or tradition.
Survivalist gangs, mercenaries, looters, and warbands who thrive in chaos and conflict.
Tribal societies ranging from peaceful natives and spiritualists to aggressive nomads and warbands.

Greaser culture is a prominent subculture, characterized by a love for classic power armor, rock 'n' roll music, and a rebellious attitude agsinst authority. Their motto: "Every Man a King". Greasers often form tight-knit communities, embracing a distinctive style and way of life that sets them apart from mainstream society.

Indentured servitude is a common practice, where individuals enter into contracts to work for a set period in exchange for passage, shelter, or other necessities. While not as severe as outright slavery, it often leads to harsh working conditions and limited freedoms.

The trade of Jet, a highly addictive drug, is a significant aspect of the local economy. Various factions and individuals engage in the production, distribution, and sale of Jet, often leading to conflicts and power struggles. The drug's popularity among the populace creates a lucrative market, but also contributes to social issues such as addiction and crime.

The fight for mutant rights is a significant social issue. Mutants often face discrimination and prejudice, leading to movements advocating for their equal treatment and integration into society. These movements seek to challenge societal norms and promote acceptance of mutants as valuable members of the community.
Mutants are seen as rightful rulers of the wastes, stronger than baseline humanity and entitled to command lesser laborers beneath them.

There are factions that advocate for the eradication of mutants, viewing them as a threat to humanity. These groups often engage in violent actions and campaigns to eliminate mutants from society, leading to widespread fear and tension between mutants and non-mutants.
Mutants are often enslaved and used for labor or entertainment. Their unique abilities make them valuable assets, but they are frequently subjected to harsh treatment and discrimination.

Why waste crops on feeding animals when you could be making booze? Nukashiners often operate in secret, using makeshift stills and brewing equipment to create their products. Their creations are highly sought after, providing both a source of income and a means of escape from the harsh realities of life in the wasteland.

The Railroad is a secretive organization dedicated to the abolition of slavery. They operate covertly, helping slaves escape to freedom and providing safe havens for those in need. Their members are highly skilled and deeply committed to their cause, often risking their lives to fight against the oppressive systems that perpetuate slavery.
A protestant-style order built around scripture and the travelling pulpit; it prizes scripture, preaching and humble worship.
A restorationist movement that mixes frontier theocracy and strict social order, inspired by old doctrinal texts.
A new cult that treats radiation and atomic remnants as sacred, with ritualised exposure and offerings.
A Lovecraftian cult venerating Ug-Qualtoth and the forerunners; rituals here are strange and alien to most.
A cathedral-centered faith with strong civic influence and ceremonial public worship.
A secular movement born from the Old World's collapse; its followers have sworn to help those in need.
A technospiritual faith devoted to the alignment of cosmic energies and the pursuit of mental purification through the Hub.

An order devoted to the law of Karma - the eternal cycle of cause and consequence. Through mindful action and reflection, followers seek to purify their deeds and restore harmony to the world's moral balance after the collapse of civilisation.
Indigenous faith rooted in ancestral spirits and the land; rituals, totems and seasonal ceremonies hold communities together.
A Catholic-style order deeply devoted to Mary and the saints, keeping older sacramental practices alive.

The Shi follow the Way of the Emperor - a philosophical order born from the fusion of ancient wisdom and new science. Knowledge, discipline, and harmony are sacred principles. They seek balance between machine, mind, and nature, guided by the teachings of the Old World.
A believe mixing elements of Christianity with tribal spirit worship.
Mutants are tolerated so long as they remain beneath the human social order. They can work, serve, and endure, but they are never treated as true equals.

The Survivalist Lifestyle meme emphasizes self-reliance, resourcefulness, and preparedness. Followers of this meme prioritize skills such as hunting, foraging, and crafting, believing that these abilities are essential for thriving in the harsh post-apocalyptic environment of Greenway. They often stockpile supplies and maintain a strong focus on personal and community defense.
A mutant homeland should belong to mutants alone. Their strength, endurance, and altered bodies are treated as proof that the future belongs to them, not to unaltered wastelanders.

In the harsh world, vice thrives as a means of escape from the daily struggles of survival. Gambling dens, brothels, and illicit trade are common, providing both a source of income and a way to forget the harsh realities of life in this unforgiving land.

Labor camps are a common sight, where prisoners and the unemployed are put to work on various projects. While conditions can be harsh, they provide a means of survival and a chance for redemption through hard work.
We are guided by signs in the heavens, old-world charts, and the omens they cast over the wastes.
Our leader must be obeyed as the living embodiment of the cause.
Because of a punga addiction, this person needs to regularly consume punga fruit to avoid withdrawal symptoms.
Recovery process for those suffering from cannibal vitamin deficiency syndrome.
Strong belief in maintaining a powerful connection to the Treefather, source of treeminders and pods.
Mutants are accepted as full members of the community. Their altered bodies are no barrier to belonging so long as they stand with the colony.
Mutants are seen as the natural overseers of the wastes. Their strength grants them the right to rule, especially over unmutated laborers kept beneath them.
Mutants are treated as a stain on civilization. Their presence in the colony is dangerous, degrading, and should be stamped out wherever possible.
Mutants are valued primarily as a slave caste. They are strong, dangerous, and best kept in chains where their labor can be exploited safely.
Mutants may remain inside society, but only in a lesser caste. They are useful when kept subordinate and put to work under firm supervision.
A true mutant homeland should be built by mutants and for mutants. Baseline wastelanders are tolerated only as a problem, never as rightful members of the order being created.
Belief in the sacred bond between colonist and Treefather, a mutated tree that produces treeminders and pods.
Travel to a remote destination using the Hellion. Unless you build a mooring mast, your current map and anything not on Hellion deck plating will be permanently lost. You will not be able to return.
Link with the wise Mothman spirit through meditation and ritual.

Initiate a presidential address from the presidential chair. All colonists will come and listen. If it goes well, listeners will feel inspired, and gain respect for the speaker. If it goes poorly, the address will do social damage. The outcome depends on the speaker's social abilities.
Issues a commendation to a worthy pawn.
Commissions a government rank for a worthy pawn.

Initiate a presidential address from the dais. {ORGANIZER_labelShort} is giving a presidential address from the dais. If all goes well, every ranked participant will gain a small amount of Votes, and {ORGANIZER_labelShort} will gain Votes for each ranked participant. If it goes poorly, the speech will cause social strain and {ORGANIZER_labelShort} will lose Votes for each ranked participant. The outcome depends on {ORGANIZER_labelShort}'s social abilities. Whitespring officials currently in the area will join the address and count as ranked participants.
High priest revelations enthrone a leader who binds followers, drains rivals, and rules through presence and pain.
Blacksite savant revelations channel forbidden machine-thought, insanity, and weaponized neural overload.
Doomwatcher revelations distort age, growth, and duration by clawing at the edges of time.
Ashcaller revelations weaponize fire, heat, and raw destructive energy in spectacular rituals.
Mesmerist revelations soothe minds, break panic, and flood nearby followers with unnatural calm.
Rimebinder revelations shape cold, ice, and entropy into barriers, traps, and killing frost.
Omenweaver revelations trade fortune for misfortune, tilting the balance of events through ritual gamble.
Gravecaller revelations spread dread, exploit corpses, and drag the dead back into service.
Nightstalker revelations reward ambush, darkness, and silent execution under a veil of unnatural shadow.
Wardkeeper revelations shield allies, reinforce flesh, and drag researchers out of fatal danger.
Gatewalker revelations tear short-lived holes through space, moving people and objects by occult shortcut.
Stormcaller revelations command lightning, blackout machinery, and turn weather into a weapon.
Relic engineer revelations wake dead circuitry, empower scavenged technology, and stitch occult insight into hardware.
Battle medium revelations are selfish war rites that sharpen reflexes, speed, and killing instinct.
Totem speaker revelations call on beasts, brambles, and shrine-bound wilderness spirits for survival and defense.
Blood sage revelations burn hemogen as ritual fuel, turning blood into pain, sacrifice, and predatory miracles.
Idol puppeteer revelations break minds, enslave wills, and turn hollowed victims into extensions of the occultist.
Blend broc flower and carrot flower into a preserved seasoning mix fit for Lucky 38 kitchens.
Render down maize or xander root into a thick preserved syrup suited for Lucky 38 desserts and bar stock.
Render down carrots into a thick preserved syrup suited for Lucky 38 desserts and bar stock.
Roll coyote tobacco chew into cigarettes.
Roll coyote tobacco chew into cigars with a bit of gold trim for a more refined smoke.
Make a fine atomic cocktail, mixed precisely from multiple ingredients.
Make a gourmet atomic cocktail where every factor has been calculated for maximum flavor and body.
Make a lavish atomic cocktail mixed from a wide variety of ingredients and chilled to perfection before serving.
Make a simple atomic cocktail quickly mixed from a duo of ingredients.
Make guarana by fortifying homebrew sarsaparilla with caffeine and a radioactive kick. It delivers fast energy and a later crash.
Make homebrew sarsaparilla syrup by reducing fruit juice and sweetener into a thick bottling concentrate.
Make homebrew sarsaparilla syrup by mixing natural fruit juice with sugar or preserved syrup. This recipe is much more efficient.
Create hot sauce from peppers or jalapenos. It keeps the same throat-burning kick while fitting the Lucky 38 pantry better.
Create ketchup from tomatoes or tatos. A thick sauce with the same sweet tang Lucky 38 diners expect from the pre-war original.
Create brahmin shroom butter from milk and smokeleaf. Rich, fatty, and wildly irresponsible, it is the sort of condiment only a place like the Lucky 38 would serve on purpose.
Craft a Liberty Prime. This is an incredibly rare and expensive unit.
Craft a RobCo Mister Gutsy Gunner.
Craft a Securitron Mark I security platform.
Craft an improved Securitron Mark II with non-lethal electroshock system.
draw rot gut from rot gut barrels
Craft ritual clothing, armor, and focus gear suited to occultists and their cryptic revelations.
Recover and tailor advanced relic-woven apparel, staves, and focus gear that amplify cryptid insight.
Build Wattz heaters and Appalachia Freeze coolers to keep rooms livable in any season, or to maintain proper freezer temperatures.
Build compact Poseidon home reactors that burn bioferrite and drive enormous amounts of heat into the surrounding air.
Develop Poseidon battery banks for storing reserve grid power with the sort of reassuring pre-War safety language that usually meant the opposite.
Build power generators that burn bioferrite as a fuel. Bioferrite keeps its own identity here, but the hardware around it can still read like a desperate late-era Poseidon project.
Build refinery equipment that converts biological feedstock into oil for generators, burners, and industrial systems.
Build Corvega deep drills for extracting resources from underground. You will still need a ground-penetrating scanner to find the best seams.
Produce Radiation King flatscreen televisions for greater comfort and entertainment.
Construct Med-Tek hospital beds that improve medical outcomes and turn a rough frontier ward into something closer to a pre-War clinic.
Work with advanced microelectronics, unlocking Wattz utility electronics, Radiation King entertainment systems, and more sophisticated industrial control equipment.
Build industrial food dispensers that turn organic feedstocks into edible paste with efficient, relentlessly impersonal pre-War engineering.
Build Poseidon Civilian Helios Two solar panels for domestic and settlement-scale electricity generation.
Produce Radiation King tube televisions for recreational watching and proper retro-futurist living room culture.
Develop pre-War Poseidon battery units capable of storing large amounts of energy with minimal loss.
Recover advanced Poseidon generation hardware, including solar arrays, hydro units, and high-capacity turbine systems.
Construct basic Corvega works, including conveyors, underground transfer points, and factory hoppers for automating routine industrial tasks around the colony.
Construct specialized Corvega factory systems capable of manufacturing components, producing alloy stock, and handling other advanced automated production jobs.
Develop heavy-duty Poseidon generator systems built to store more fuel and feed more power into the grid.
Establish larger Corvega production benches and powered industrial work centers for high-throughput fabrication, assembly, and finishing.
Restore the harshest end of the pre-War power ladder: compact reactor systems with enormous output and equally severe failure risks.
Build Poseidon tidal generators on open water to supply steady industrial power from current and surf.
Research propane deposits and the pipe-fed burner systems that let it function as a compact industrial fuel.
Develop Med-Tek patient telemetry rigs that improve surgery outcomes and long-term recovery when mounted beside a hospital bed.
Develop basic waste recovery systems for turning toxic refuse into usable industrial feedstock.
Expand waste recovery into sealed reclamation lines for alloy, isotope, and compacted refuse products.
Build a reliable climate-control system connecting Wattz control hardware to Appalachia Freeze cooling equipment and related ventilation components.
Build Poseidon hydro generators on rivers to generate a steady supply of power.
Build advanced Hellion upgrades and support systems.
Rebuild the Hellion and use it to travel the planet. Without a mooring mast, launching the Hellion will abandon your current map.
Build more complex Hellion components and flight systems.
Construct astrofuel synthesizers that automatically convert chemfuel into astrofuel for Hellion use.
Unlock smaller variants of workbenches designed for the Hellion. Compact enough to fit into tight quarters without sacrificing essential functions.
Build Hellion-exclusive furniture such as bunk beds, lockers, and medbay beds to keep crew comfortable and functional during long voyages.
Construct Hellion solar panelling, reactor-core power cells, and astrofuel generators to supply energy for Hellion systems.
Construct Hellion artillery and point-defense turrets to fight hostile craft in orbit or provide bombardment from the sky.
Harvest structural heat generated during Hellion launches and dissipate it safely. Excess heat can be converted into usable power.
Harvest and store oxygen for use on the Hellion. Unlocks construction of vacsuits and other survival gear needed to operate in vacuum.
Unlocks the secrets of pre-war RobCo robotics. Required for all further RobCo robot research.
Unlocks advanced RobCo robot types and upgrades.
Enables construction and control of standard RobCo robots.
Unlocks the most powerful and secret RobCo robots.
Learn to cultivate wasteland ingredients and brew different extracts at an extract lab.
Research into advanced treatments for the Wendigo Shakes.
Recover West Tek tissue processing methods and build the extraction hardware needed to break subjects down into usable strain templates.
Duplicate proven strain templates and preserve valuable West Tek genomes for repeated use.
Use West Tek vats, incubation tanks, and booster benches to assemble new test organisms and forced-evolution prototypes.
Create Whitespring megaturrets: Kontarion, Onager and Palintone, as well as their ammunition.
Craft powerful Whitespring mortar shells, such as shrapnel, armor piercing and cluster.
Craft heavy trooper armor, light powered armor thatâ€™s commonly used by Whitespring recon troops. Note that these also require advanced components.
Build presidential decor like Whitespring rugs, mirrors, grand chairs, Whitespring slabs, candelabras, and more.
Construct a presidential desk chair suited for senior Whitespring officials and their ceremonial duties.
Quality factors determine the reactor's cooldown duration and the chance for a mishap when landing.
At the current quality, the Hellion will not be able to launch again for {0_time} after landing.
Additionally, there is a {0_percentage} chance of having a minor incident when landing the Hellion.
Upon completion of the parade, the vice president and their chosen companions will formally enter executive service to the Whitespring Enclave.

Sergeant is a non-commissioned officer rank, responsible for leading squads, maintaining discipline, and ensuring the effectiveness of Enclave operations. Sergeants are often promoted from the ranks for their leadership and technical skills.

Colonel is a senior officer rank, commanding regiments and managing large-scale strategic operations. Colonels are responsible for the training, discipline, and combat readiness of their units within the Appalachia Division.

Attorney General is a top-level legal and strategic advisor, responsible for interpreting Enclave law, overseeing military justice, and advising on matters of policy and command. This rank is reserved for the most trusted officers.

General is a high command rank in the Enclave, overseeing multiple regiments and directing major campaigns. Generals are responsible for strategic planning and the overall success of Enclave operations in Appalachia.

Secretary is a senior administrative rank, coordinating logistics, intelligence, and support services for the Enclave's military branches. Secretaries ensure the smooth operation of all divisions and facilitate communication with high command.

President is the supreme commander of the Enclave, holding ultimate authority over all divisions, personnel, and operations. The President embodies the ideals of the prewar United States and leads the Enclave in its mission to restore order and civilization.

Member is the entry-level rank for recognized personnel in the Enclave's Appalachia Division. Most earn this status through basic service, training, or acts that demonstrate loyalty to the Enclave's mission and values.

Lieutenant is a junior officer rank in the Enclave, typically commanding platoons or serving as executive officers. Lieutenants are entrusted with tactical leadership and the execution of critical missions in the field.

Captain is a commissioned officer rank, responsible for leading companies and overseeing major operations. Captains are experienced leaders who coordinate missions and ensure the Enclave's objectives are met efficiently.

Vice-President is a national-level executive rank, acting as deputy to the President and overseeing the coordination of all Enclave operations across multiple regions. The Vice-President ensures the Enclave's mission is carried out with unity and precision.
The Whitespring rank of general is reserved for senior commanders who have built the infrastructure needed to host inspections, briefings, and high-level visitors.

The Whitespring title of senator is commissioned to secretaries whose jurisdiction requires a larger command structure to maintain order. Senators are expected to field and train their own divisions and, in peacetime, expand the Enclave's regional administrative reach.

The Whitespring title of minister maintains authority over a strategically important system. In the Whitespring Enclave, ministers ensure that resources continue to flow into Whitespring stockpiles and factories while their offices hold key administrative positions throughout the system of government.

The Whitespring title of president represents supreme executive authority within the Whitespring Enclave. The office answers to no higher power and is expected not only to maintain control, but to expand the Enclave's influence wherever possible. Very few are capable of receiving this title, and even fewer wish to take on its responsibilities.

The Whitespring title of state secretary is the middle rank of high government. In the Whitespring Enclave, a state secretary is expected to coordinate the settlement's vast military capabilities in both peace and wartime, operating directly under the president.

The Whitespring title of attorney general is one of the highest ranks in the middle government. In the Whitespring Enclave, attorney generals oversee frontier territories and protect the edge of Whitespring space. During wartime, they can field fleets that either hold the line or support broader offensive operations.

Private is the first operational rank in the Enclave's military structure, granted to those who have completed initial training and are entrusted with basic duties and responsibilities within the Appalachia Division.

Call a light trooper to assist you for 4 days. This specialist is a master assassin and will not perform any tasks other than killing. You can control them as though they were one of your own colonists. If a light trooper dies, a replacement will arrive immediately and inherit the remaining contract time.
Call an orbital beam from a Whitespring flagship in orbit. The beam will travel in a direction specified by you, burning everything it touches.
Call a group of 6 presidential guards, ranged and melee, to aid you in battle.
Call a scientist to assist you for 4 days. This specialist can only perform research-related tasks. You can control him as though he were one of your own colonists. You are required to keep him safe.

Your airship tore itself apart over the wasteland and three of you barely made it down alive. The wreck scattered supplies across the desert, and now you have one chance to turn an airship crash site into a home.

Your tribe was burned out by steel war machines and only five of you escaped with what you could carry. Now you must keep the old ways alive long enough to raise a new home from dust, bone, and scrap.\n\nNote: You begin poor, hungry, and far behind the technology of the wastes. This is a difficult scenario.

Someone stripped you, robbed you, and dumped you naked in the wasteland to die. No gear, no food, no allies, not even a knife. If you live through this, it will be because you refused to become carrion.\n\nWarning: This scenario is brutally unfair. Death can come fast and for stupid reasons.

You were born behind a vault door, surrounded by safety, soft beds, and old-world lies. Instead of staying buried, you took your best gear and went looking for the real wasteland. Now your pod has dropped you far from any friendly road, alone with vault supplies and no one coming to save you.\n\nNote: Strong gear cannot cover every weakness. This is an especially difficult early game scenario.

Your Helixen, a pre-war REPCONN aircraft, sits docked at the orbital relay. As your crew begins salvaging its systems, you realize this is no ordinary pre-war cache - it is a robot beacon. The relay crackles to life and broadcasts a war alert to a distant robot command hub. They are hunting you now, and they will eventually find you wherever you go.\n\nNote: This is a difficult scenario and is not recommended for new players.

You are a RobCo mechanic, trained to wake old-world robots and bend them to useful work. People are unreliable; machines are honest. You arrived in the wasteland with a control rig, a pocketful of salvage, and a small crew of utility bots ready to build something new.\n\nNote: This scenario is challenging because your early strength is tied to robotics rather than raw manpower.
This start has been disabled by Sunset.
This Settlers scenario has been disabled by Sunset.

The old stories called creatures like you Wendigos: starving things that outlived the people they once were. However it happened, you carry that curse now. Hunted, half-feral, and never truly full, you fled into the wastes before the wrong people could pin you down.\n\nNote: Wendigos have unique strengths and brutal weaknesses, making this a difficult scenario.

Your Brotherhood expeditionary group has stumbled upon an old bunker, a potential haven. But with the valuable intel you carry, the Whitespring Enclave's visibility on you is alarmingly high. Vertibots patrol the skies, and Whitespring raiding parties are closing in. Use the intel to unlock powerful tech and Brotherhood expeditionary services, aiding your ultimate mission: to assassinate key Whitespring officials. Survive the initial onslaught and lay low; over time, the Whitespring Enclave's relentless search will wane. The choice is yours: fortify, adapt, or perish.\n\nNote: Since you start with high visibility, this is a difficult scenario.

It was supposed to be another quiet night for your Whitespring splinter cell, tucked away in a secluded estate far from the main administrative halls. You do not know whether the blackout was caused by Brotherhood sabotage, a compromised security officer, or a simple systems failure, but when the perimeter defenses died at midnight your enclave cell was left blind and exposed. By the time the gates opened, there was no time to organize a proper response - only to hide and survive.\nYou heard your officers, researchers, and support staff being cut down as the Brotherhood expeditionary strike team swept through the compound. Whatever remained of your old command structure died with them. Now your splinter cell is shattered, your safehouse is burning, and revenge will mean nothing unless you escape this raid alive. You can wait the assault out, use permits to call for reinforcements, or try running for the hills - the choice is yours.\nNote: Since you start during a raid in progress, this is a difficult scenario.
The reactor-core signal is coming from somewhere in this settlement. Search the abandoned buildings to recover the core.
An ancient reactor is hidden within nearby ruins. The reactor is cracked and leaking toxic fallout over the whole region. Locate and disable it to obtain a reactor core and stop the fallout.

You have detected the signal of a reactor core emanating from an ancient underground stockpile. The location is marked on the world map.\n\nRecover reactor cores and airframe panels to expand your Hellion.
The remains of an orbital platform are strewn nearby. Be careful, the wreckage contains hostile robots.\n\nThe reactor-core signal is coming from somewhere within the crash site.

You have detected the signal of a reactor core. The core is powering a malfunctioning ancient terraformer. Its location is marked on the world map.

The terraformer is cooling the local region to dangerously low temperatures. Be prepared for cold weather.

Recover reactor cores and airframe panels to expand your Hellion.
The crash site of a Hellion prototype.

You have detected the signal of a reactor core emanating from a cave system deep underground. The entrance is marked on the world map.\n\nRecover reactor cores and airframe panels to upgrade your Hellion.
The reactor-core signal is coming from a robot communication relay here. Disassemble it to acquire the reactor core.

You have detected the signal of a reactor core.\n\nBe careful - orbital locations are extremely dangerous. Ensure your Hellion is fully enclosed with airtight walls and equip your colonists with vacsuits. Plan your return trip, as living in orbit for long periods is very difficult.

You have detected the signal of a reactor core on this platform. There is a heavy robot presence.\n\nBe careful, orbital locations are extremely dangerous. Ensure your Hellion is fully enclosed with airtight walls and equip your colonists with vacsuits. Plan your return trip, as living in orbit for long periods is very difficult.
An ancient RobCo facility filled with pre-war robotics technology and secrets. Might contain a useable Pipboy.
A looted and abandoned caravan company camp.
A dead drop from our Brotherhood expeditionary contact.
The former flagship of the Whitespring Enclave, now crashed.
A downed vertibird carrying a senior Whitespring official.
A secret Whitespring installation equipped with a large number of powerful orbital cannons, capable of bringing down large objects from the orbit.
How quickly a colonist can prune the Treefather, maintaining its connection strength and treeminder production.
The maximum distance this Hellion can travel with its connected deck plating.
The maximum amount of Hellion deck plating supported by this Hellion.
How much bandwidth this robot consumes when under mechanic control.
How many seconds it will take for a mechanic to take control of this mech.

How much bandwidth a robot has. More bandwidth allows a mechanic to produce and control more robots at the same time.\n\nLosing bandwidth means losing control of robots, leaving them without an overseer. Such mechs can eventually become feral if not re-dominated.
How many control groups this mechanic can direct independently. Each control group can be given its own separate orders.
The amount of energy that this robot loses for every 100 damage repaired.
A multiplier on how fast a robot consumes its energy reserves while operating.
A multiplier on the speed at which this mechanic can create new robots at a assembler.
How far away a robot can be during remote repair.
The maximum range at which a mechanic can place a shield on a robot.
The energy a remote shield will have when created by a mechanic.
A multiplier on how fast a mechanic can repair robots.
A multiplier on how fast a mechanic can solder circuitry.
How many toxic wastepacks this robot will generate at a RobCo recharger when going from 0 to 100% energy. Larger robots will generally create more toxic wastepacks.
Diego Dire believes the best stories are the violent ones, facing unfavorable odds and going down in a blaze of glory with exploding pack beasts and drunks.

Damocles knows that the only way to defeat the Whitespring Enclave is to tread the fine line between all out war and espionage. Good events only come to those who fight. The story that unfolds will be that of increasing tension and desperation, focused on a small group of likeminded people surviving the onslaught together.

A lightweight structural deck that forms the floor of the Hellion. Only things resting on this plating can be carried by the aircraft, and it must be built within range of the Hellion reactor.\n\nHellion deck plating can be removed using the 'remove foundation' designator.

A rare, mutated fruit found in the wasteland. Punga fruit is prized for its unique taste and the mild, uplifting high it provides. Consuming too much can lead to a subtle addiction, but survivors swear by its mood-boosting effects.
A stasis cocoon containing a fire ant.
A stasis cocoon containing a fire soldier ant.
A stasis cocoon containing a fire worker ant.
A fire ant adapted for underground life. Its digging claws and hive coordination make it a formidable tunnel dweller.
A winged fire ant bred for mating flights and scouting. On its own it is fragile, but swarms of drones are still dangerous.
A hive of giant fire ants. If activated, it will spawn additional fire ants and hives over time, as well as valuable insect jelly.
The juvenile stage of a fire ant. Fire ant larvae are nurtured by workers and mature into the hive's next caste.
The queen of a fire ant megahive. She anchors the colony, lays the next generation, and turns any nest she survives in into a larger threat.
The most aggressive of the fire ants. These creatures defend their hive with relentless ferocity, thick chitin, and powerful jaws.
A massive fire ant engineered for strength and resilience. Fire worker ants are fiercely territorial and maintain their hive with absolute loyalty.
The queen of a giant ant hive. She is responsible for laying eggs and directing the colony's growth and defense.
The juvenile stage of a giant ant. Ant larvae are cared for by worker ants and will eventually grow into powerful hive members.
A giant ant capable of flight. Ant drones only live to mate with the queen.
A massive ant engineered for strength and resilience. Giant ants are known for their powerful mandibles and hive loyalty.
The most aggressive of the giant ants. These creatures defend their hive with relentless ferocity and powerful jaws.
A hardy desert yucca with twisted limbs and dagger leaves. In the old Mojave, these trees marked roads, water, and trouble from miles away.
A blackened, near dead tree which survived a nearby atomic blast. When harvested, it yields a meager amount of wood.
A giant ant adapted for underground life. Its tough exoskeleton and digging ability make it a formidable tunnel dweller.

A rugged Corvega APC rebuilt into an armed road escort. It trades comfort and efficiency for protection, firepower, and reliable highway travel.

It still burns through gasoline at an alarming rate, but wasteland caravans keep them running because they are cheap, fast, and mean enough to scare off light raiders.
