A lightweight pre-War Chryslus road car built for fast travel, simple cargo hauling, and staying on open pavement whenever possible.

Like most surviving wasteland automobiles, it drinks gasoline greedily and performs worst when dragged into mud, hills, or winter terrain.

<color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Driver x1, Passenger x1
<color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Gasoline

A lightweight pre-War Chryslus road car built for fast travel, simple cargo hauling, and staying on open pavement whenever possible.

Like most surviving wasteland automobiles, it drinks gasoline greedily and performs worst when dragged into mud, hills, or winter terrain.

<color=#bb8f04>Crew:</color> Driver x1, Passenger x1
<color=#bb8f04>Fuel type:</color> Gasoline

An old, rusted fragment of a prototype reactor. It was likely abandoned when Moldaver's construction program was halted. It may still be possible to disassemble it and recover valuable fusion components.

A prototype fusion core capable of generating large amounts of power from a geothermal foundation. It can later be upgraded into more advanced reactor variants once the correct research notes and hardware have been recovered. Must be placed on a steam geyser.
A twisted chunk of reactor housing from one of Moldaver's abandoned projects. The valuable parts are gone, and everything else has degraded into scrap.

A rusted military crate filled with deadlife shells. These artillery rounds are packed with a dust-like compound that settles on nearby human and animal corpses after detonation and raises them as feral ghouls and ghoul-beasts. Many of the shells have lost their potency, but some may still work.
The remains of a Treefather, chopped down and left as a stump.

A hardy caste of treeminder, the radbarkling is covered in thick, bark-like hide infused with radiation. It excels at absorbing damage in combat, protecting its tree and allies. In general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.
A basic treeminder, mammal-like and loyal to its Treefather. It can morph into specialized castes as needed.

A dryad caste specialized in food production, the mutfruitling generates nourishing mutfruit on an ongoing basis. However, it is slow and ineffective at work or combat. In general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.

A helpful caste of treeminder, the portling is specialized in carrying items. Under human influence, it hauls goods where they need to go, but is slow and ineffective at work or combat. In general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.

A fierce caste of treeminder, the scratchling is bred for aggression and damage-dealing attacks. Its oversized claws can tear through most threats, but it is vulnerable to retaliation. In general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.

A unique caste of treeminder, the sproutling can produce new Gauranlen pods and help reproduce the treefather. Three sproutlings must slowly merge to complete the process. In general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.

A gentle caste of treeminder, the brocflowerling specializes in producing medicinal herbs. It works with its tree to generate healing plants, but is slow and ineffective at work or combat. In general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.

A slow, bumbling caste of treeminder which specializes in pungs production. Together with its Gauranlen tree, this dryad can generate punga fruit on an ongoing basis. However, it is slow and ineffective at work or combat.\n\nIn general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.

A slow, bumbling caste of treeminder, the scrapwoodling specializes in producing scrap wood. Together with its Gauranlen tree, it generates wood-like material on an ongoing basis, but is slow and ineffective at work or combat. In general, treeminders are mammal-like creatures that have a symbiotic relationship with the treefather. They reproduce together with their tree, which contains FEV samples. Treeminders can morph into various specialized castes.
A cocoon where a treeminder morphs into a new caste, guided by the Treefather's FEV-infused roots.
A pod filled with restorative Treefather sap, used to heal injured treeminders.
A cocoon where sproutlings merge to create a new Treefather pod, continuing the symbiotic cycle.
A seed from the Treefather, able to grow a new mutated tree and begin the symbiotic cycle.
A patch of moss that grows around the Treefather, beautifying the area and aiding treeminder production.
A pod produced by the Treefather, containing the seeds of new treeminders.
A mutated tree infused with FEV, able to produce treeminders and pods. Its roots run deep, connecting it to the wasteland's secrets.
The remains of a Treefather, smashed and left as a stump.
A tall ceremonial headdress meant to mark the leader of an organized faith or wasteland cult.
A small, plain hat meant to rest gently on one's head. Those who favor it often claim it gives them the look of an old-world mystic.
A distinguished brimmed cap adorned with a badge of rank, meant to inspire obedience in subordinates at any cost.
A flat-topped conical hat decorated with a black tassel, often worn by desert traders, caravan bosses, and other old-road elites.
A set of worn rags meant to cover the body with minimal fuss. They offer almost no protection or warmth, but clearly signal a life of austerity and hard wasteland devotion.
Automated cargo bots used to deliver people and supplies across the planet.
A bizarre archotech signal flare that calls a vertibird out of nowhere. Whatever intelligence answers it is willing to provide one miraculous extraction.
A single-use field transponder used to request Brotherhood vertibird pickup.
A register desk for lounge service, dining tabs, and house accounts. It does nothing on its own, but it anchors the front-of-house operation.
An automated service terminal for mechanoid attendants. From here you can direct dining and front-of-house operations.
A carefully wrapped cache of pre-war chocolate. Rich, sweet, and still decadent enough to anchor desserts and syrups.
A premium Lucky 38 slot bank built for high-limit play, polished brass, and a hungry house cut.
A House-era slot bank tuned for regular patrons and reliable nightly traffic.
A reclaimed Vault 21 slot machine with a modest stake and a forgiving house edge.
A thick preserved sweetener rendered down for Lucky 38 kitchens. It fills the same role as sugar in sauces, desserts, and mixed drinks.
A low-tech kiosk for patrons that needs no electricity.
A placard used to provide context and presentation for an exhibit piece.
Designates the spot where patrons gather and wait for a guided gallery tour to begin. A room must have a tour start spot to count as an exhibit gallery.
A carefully worked hot spring built over a steam geyser. Its mineral waters offer meditative relaxation, modest recovery benefits, and a premium bathhouse amenity for up to four pawns at once.
A long-range IFF beacon and traffic-control mast used by Brotherhood cells and trusted allies to advertise a safe vertibird approach, coordinate landings, and broadcast refueling availability.

A service terminal that sells aviation fuel to Brotherhood vertibirds and other authorized aircraft. It must be placed adjacent to a vertibird pad; once payment is verified, pumps inside the pad draw fuel from tanks on the same power network.
An industrial processor that refines volatile feedstock and water into aviation fuel for Brotherhood vertibirds. It runs best with water access and turns surplus chemfuel into saleable flight stock.
A chilled storage tank for processed aviation fuel. Keep it powered or the stock begins to evaporate away.

A hardened landing pad with guidance lights, fueling hookups, and an encrypted transponder for Brotherhood vertibirds. Keep it powered and clear, and visiting patrols, quartermasters, traders, and allied passengers can touch down here.
Designates a configurable point where visiting Brotherhood personnel, traders, and allied passengers will gather after landing. If no valid point is found, they will remain near their vertibird.
Help colonists and patrons work through stress, trauma, and dependency in a private counseling suite.
Also known as liquid sunshine, pungashine is a refined liquor distilled from ripe punga fruit. It is rare, expensive, and prized anywhere a properly stocked bar still exists.
A gilded distillery of very delicate craftsmanship meant for aging pungashine must into a drinkable form.
Sliced and pressed punga fruit ready to ripen toward pungashine.
Fully ripened punga liquor base, ready to be distilled into pungashine.
A small, rolled paper tube stuffed loosely with coyote tobacco chew. It offers a brief euphoria, mild stimulation, and the same stale wasteland smell that keeps smokers coming back.
A thick cigar rolled from coyote tobacco chew and finished for a more expensive, more deliberate smoke. It carries a rough desert bitterness under the fancy presentation.
A sharp, fizzy bottle of guarana finished with bitterness and a radioactive aftertaste. It hits hard up front and leaves a crash waiting on the other side.
A fine atomic cocktail, mixed precisely from multiple ingredients.
A miracle of mixology where every glass, garnish, and stir has been tuned into a gourmet atomic cocktail.
A lavish atomic cocktail mixed from a wide variety of ingredients and chilled to perfection before being served.
Bright carrot flowers used to freshen brewed drinks and garnish wasteland dishes.
A stand of carrot flowers used for bright herbal garnish in teas and coffee blends.
A hardy stand of coyote tobacco suited to the Mojave and other dry wasteland climates. Once cured down, the leaves become coyote tobacco chew for smoking, rolling, or trading.
Vacuum-sealed tins of pre-war instant coffee. Bitter, shelf-stable, and still potent enough to anchor every Lucky 38 breakfast service.
Dried pre-war tea leaves stored in a Lucky 38 pantry tin. They keep well and still steep into a proper serviceable cup.
A cured pouch of coyote tobacco chew. Dry enough to smoke, roll, or pack into richer blends, with the dusty bite wasteland smokers expect.
A simple atomic cocktail quickly mixed from a duo of ingredients.
A sweet, fizzy bottle of homebrew sarsaparilla. It is more refreshing than nutritious, but a chilled bottle still feels like a pre-war luxury.
An all-in-one homebrew sarsaparilla bottling unit. Syrup is mixed, carbonated, chilled, and dispensed into sterilized bottles for service.
A thick, intensely sweet base for homebrew sarsaparilla. It is meant to be cut, carbonated, and bottled before serving.
A rare pre-war medical suspension repackaged for the Lucky 38 kitchen. When folded into food, aqua cura floods the body with emergency repair nanites that can drag a diner back from death.
Tart wasteland cranberries. They can be eaten raw, cooked into preserves, or fermented into a thick table sauce.
A preserved blend of pre-war kitchen spices sealed away in a Lucky 38 pantry tin. Enough survives inside each can to brighten stews, sauces, and grills.
Sealed packets of pre-war sugar recovered from old kitchens and pantry caches. It keeps indefinitely and still delivers the same quick rush of sweetness.
A dense, intoxicating butter churned for indulgent Lucky 38 cooking. It spreads rich, hits hard, and turns any meal into a bad idea with excellent flavor.
A hardy cranberry bush cultivated for sharp red berries. The fruit cooks down into a rich sauce that stands in for the old-world fermented condiment chain.
A rich cranberry reduction used as a dip, glaze, and salty-sweet finishing sauce for sushi and wasteland seafood.
A patch of xander root prized for its pungent bite. When ground into paste it makes a strong condiment for seafood and rice dishes.
A sharp paste made from ground xander root and used as a pungent condiment for sushi and other seafood dishes.

A battered cookbook packed with reclaimed recipes, ingredient notes, and pre-war kitchen tricks.\n\nOnce read, it will give the reader a new favorite ingredient or meal, then fall apart from one too many hands in the kitchen.
A pre-War kiosk stocked for patrons, casino traffic, and idle lounge crowds.
A Poseidon home reactor powered by scavenged dark archotechnology. It burns bioferrite to agitate atmospheric gases, raising the outdoor temperature while flooding the area with waste heat.
A Poseidon reserve battery for holding excess electricity. Charged units remain dangerous when exposed to rain or fire, regardless of what the brochure claims.

An experimental generator that works by pressurizing and igniting bioferrite, then harvesting energy from the resulting heat. The fuel remains bioferrite, but the chassis and control layout read like a harsh Poseidon prototype.

A specialized shaping bench that uses heat and electromagnetic stimulation to temporarily coax bioferrite into a pliable state. The material remains bioferrite, but the surrounding hardware fits cleanly into a Fallout-style industrial workshop.
A Poseidon refinery that converts biological matter into oil for generators, burners, and industrial storage systems.
A refined liquid fuel suitable for generators, burners, and industrial feed systems. In a saner age Poseidon would have sold it through a smiling subsidiary with a patriotic slogan.
Produces power by consuming oil. It must be periodically refueled by hand unless your settlement has the industrial habits of an actual Poseidon facility.
A Wattz communications console for contacting other factions and orbital traders. Big, expensive, and full of humming pre-War electronics.
An Appalachia Freeze cooling unit that pushes cold air to one side and hot exhaust to the other. It can preserve food or simply make summer tolerable if the rest of the grid holds.
A Corvega deep-crust drilling rig for extracting minerals from far below the surface. Use a ground-penetrating scanner to detect the best seams.
A powered industrial chem lab with containers, heaters, and measurement devices for efficient batch processing of drugs and compounds.
A heavy industrial crematorium that vaporizes corpses at extreme temperatures. It is efficient, impersonal, and unmistakably pre-War in temperament.
A Corvega reclamation smelter that extracts usable metal from slag and mixed scrap. It consumes a lot of power and throws off serious industrial heat.
A Corvega fabrication bench equipped with advanced tools for producing sophisticated machines and components from simpler materials.

A Med-Tek intravenous drip stand for rapidly delivering lost fluids to a patient, helping immunity gain and blood loss recovery when placed directly adjacent to a hospital bed or surgical table. Only works for hospital beds or surgical tables; normal beds will not benefit. Attaching more than one stand to the same bed has no effect.
A large Med-Tek centre for monitoring the vitals of several patients at once, improving tend quality, surgery success, and immunity gain when linked to hospital beds or surgical suites.
A Wattz safety device that sprays fire-retardant foam when it detects a blaze. Exactly the kind of sensible utility hardware that survives long after the corporation that sold it is gone.
A Radiation King flatscreen television with crystal-clear image and rich color. Trust the king for all your TV and radio needs.
A Poseidon geothermal plant that taps deep heat for steady, high-output power. It is exactly the kind of hulking civic infrastructure Poseidon loved to dominate a skyline with.
A Wattz Electronics heating unit that converts electricity into controlled room heat. It automatically cycles to reach a target temperature.
A Wattz high-tech research bench with computers and electronic measurement equipment. It enables rapid research and more advanced technical projects.
A Med-Tek hospital bed specially designed for clinical care. Adjustable support geometry and integrated instruments improve patient outcomes over ordinary beds.
A huge Radiation King television for settlements that want their entertainment loud, bright, and impossible to ignore.
An industrial dispenser that synthesizes edible nutrient paste from feedstocks placed in adjacent hoppers. Efficient, joyless, and exactly the sort of machine a large contractor would call progress.
A Wattz orbital trade beacon used to mark goods for exchange with orbital merchants. Dependable, compact, and built for practical frontier commerce.
A bundle of utility cables for moving power around a settlement. It can be placed under walls and other structures as part of a proper pre-War grid.
An industrial storage tank meant for oil delivered through the pipe network. Tanks can be siphoned for usable fuel as needed, but even light damage can turn leaking vapors into a violent fireball.
A floor-mounted Wattz lamp that lights an area using electricity. It is less powerful than a standing lamp, but it looks right at home in a retro-futurist sitting room.
A Med-Tek serum lab that uses scavenged archotechnology to create single-use injectable serums. Clean, expensive, and morally questionable in exactly the right pre-War way.
A Poseidon Civilian Helios Two solar panel array for settlement-scale power generation. It delivers its best output under full daylight and clear skies.
An outdoor utility lamp with an integrated Poseidon solar collector. It charges by day and provides dependable lighting after dark.
A Radiation King tube television built around bulky old display hardware and a distinctly pre-War sense of domestic prestige.

A compact power cell that generates free energy forever without conventional fuel. Even by pre-War standards it feels less like engineering and more like a secret Poseidon program nobody was meant to understand.
A basin meant to feed stored oil into the network and fill connected tanks.
Steel pipes designed to move usable oil from deepchem refineries to oil tanks for storage or to oil-fed structures for automatic refilling.
A basin meant to hold dispensed oil from storage tanks for later use.
Switches oil flow on or off.
Buried steel pipes designed to move usable oil from deepchem refineries to oil tanks for storage or to oil-fed structures for automatic refilling.
A sealed Poseidon battery unit with far better efficiency than common storage cells. These pre-War housings are rainproof and less prone to catastrophic discharge.
A high-efficiency Poseidon solar array marketed as the Industrial Helios Two, built to pull significantly more charge from direct sunlight than ordinary civilian panels.
A Poseidon hydro unit designed to draw industrial power from moving water. Crowded installations create turbulence and reduce output.

A pre-War Poseidon utility turbine built for industrial output. It needs broad clear approaches to keep airflow stable and advertises the sort of oversized confidence Poseidon loved to stamp on its public works.
A powered Corvega assembly bench fitted for turning raw stock into industrial and spacer-grade components.
A fuel-fed foundry station for recovering usable metal from slag and scrap items.
A large sealed Poseidon battery bank built for dependable utility storage. Unlike older packs, these housings withstand rain without exploding.
A large Poseidon battery bank meant to hold reserve power for later demand. Fully charged units are dangerous when exposed to rain or fire.
A compact reserve battery for local circuits and small industrial loads. Charged units can still blow when exposed to rain or fire.
A motorized butcher line with a rotary saw for fast, precise carcass breakdown. It consumes a heavy amount of power.
A powered Corvega chem lab with heaters, vessels, and gauges for efficient batch processing of compounds and drugs.
A large Corvega machining platform with advanced tooling and floor space for rapid fabrication of technical equipment. It consumes power.
A powered Corvega forge station for weapons, tools, and heavy benchwork. It draws a substantial load from the grid.
An industrial masonry saw with powered cutters for turning chunks into building blocks at assembly-line speed.
A large industrial cooking line with multiple ovens and burners for rapid meal production under heavy settlement demand.
A large industrial tailoring bench with powered sewing equipment for rapid clothing output. It can still limp along without power at reduced speed.
A large Corvega field machine that handles sowing and harvest cycles across a designated crop zone, then pushes the yield into attached works hoppers.

An automated Corvega refinery that converts wood and other organic feedstock into oil. It requires a factory floor, works hoppers, and can be overclocked for higher output at higher heat and power cost.

An automated Corvega deep extraction rig for pulling stone and buried metals up from the crust. Works machinery needs hoppers for intake and output, throws off serious heat, and can be overclocked at the cost of even greater draw and temperature.
A Corvega masonry line for turning stone chunks into consistent construction blocks. It needs hoppers, throws heat, and responds well to overclocking if your grid can support it.
A heavy Corvega smelter for recovering metal from slag, scrap gear, stone, and mechanoid remains. It belongs on a factory floor and can be overclocked for harsher but faster reclamation.
A basic industrial conveyor for moving materials between work cells with minimal labor handling.
Stores feedstock and finished goods for automated Corvega works machinery.
A buried intake point that lets an industrial conveyor run beneath short stretches of ground before resurfacing.
A buried discharge point that brings an underground conveyor back to the surface near the next work cell.
A compact Wattz Electronics air scrubber that filters smoke, dust, and hazardous particulates while improving room cleanliness and indoor air quality.

A large ultratech shield projector with the unmistakable feel of a Poseidon black-budget installation. It consumes enormous power while active and can set the surrounding area ablaze if it absorbs too much punishment too quickly.

A compact ultratech shield projector that reads like one of Poseidon's more secretive defense contracts. It throws up a potent energy field when a threat is present and idles at far lower draw the rest of the time.

A specialized Corvega anti-air turret that intercepts explosive projectiles and incoming drop pods within its defensive radius. It cannot engage ground targets, but it looks every bit like a pre-War perimeter solution.

An armored portable turret with Corvega targeting electronics and reinforced plating. Extra armor helps it survive incoming fire, but the control package remains just dumb enough to be dangerous around friendlies.
A twin-barreled autocannon turret with Corvega fire-control electronics. It trades finesse for volume of fire and remains a power-hungry, violently practical defensive platform.

A portable flamer turret with Corvega control electronics and a brutally simple fuel system. It can engulf enemies and terrain in fire, and it remains as unsafe as any honest pre-War weapon platform should be.
A refined automated turret with Corvega targeting hardware, military-grade stabilization, and hardened casing. Its dumb AI brain still cannot be directly controlled, so beware of friendly fire.
A heavy automatic turret whose multi-barrel mechanism unleashes sustained fire at high speed. Corvega control systems keep it cycling, but they do not make it subtle.
A Corvega searchlight combining an intensely bright lamp with a mirrored reflector, useful for illuminating and dazzling targets across a defensive perimeter.
A compact automatic turret fitted with a semi-automatic shotgun and Corvega targeting electronics. Short-ranged, nasty, and still not smart enough to trust completely.
A tall propane floodlight that illuminates a massive area. It is not bright enough for crop growth, but it provides dependable yard lighting when connected to the propane network.
A propane-fed standing lamp for utility lighting. It must be connected to the propane network and can be placed safely outdoors.
An industrial lamp bright enough for crop growth, but it burns a steady supply of propane. It shuts itself off at night.
A propane-fed wall light for utility corridors and work rooms. It must be connected to the propane network and can be placed safely outdoors.
A Med-Tek patient telemetry unit that improves tend quality, surgery success, and recovery when mounted directly beside a hospital bed.
A bedside Med-Tek feeding device that delivers nutrient paste directly to bedridden patients when linked to a nutrient vat. Practical, clinical, and just unsettling enough to feel authentic.
A compact Corvega feeder that outputs nutrient paste from the pipe network for routine worker consumption.
A Corvega nutrient grinder that reduces organic feedstocks into pipeline-ready paste stock. Efficient, impersonal, and very much in the spirit of industrial pre-War food engineering.
A Corvega paste tap that dispenses prepared nutrient meals from the vat network. It is the kind of practical cafeteria hardware a large contractor would install by the hundred.
A Corvega storage vat for holding large volumes of processed nutrient paste. Destroying one makes exactly the kind of industrial mess you would expect.
A large Poseidon groundwater reclamation pump that gradually strips pollution from broad terrain and condenses the recovered waste into haulable toxic packs.
A compact high-draw reclamation pump that clears polluted terrain quickly by burning a heavy amount of grid power and packing the recovered contaminants into toxic wastepacks.
A sealed industrial locker for holding toxic waste products and reclaimed hazardous packs without letting them leak into the local environment.
A large Appalachia Freeze wall cooler for serious refrigeration and industrial climate control. Cold air exits one side while hot exhaust is dumped out the other.
A large Wattz industrial heater that fits into a wall and pushes serious utility heat. It can automatically cycle to a target temperature.
A large Wattz ceiling fan for workshops, warehouses, and other rooms that need stronger air circulation.
A Wattz ceiling fan for slow, steady air circulation in smaller rooms.
A freestanding Appalachia Freeze cooling unit for open rooms that need relief from summer heat without full freezer performance.
A Wattz wall heater that fits into a wall and keeps rooms warm without wasting floor space.
An Appalachia Freeze wall-mounted cooler for compact freezer rooms and tight utility spaces.
A compact Wattz wall-mounted heater for utility corridors, workshops, and smaller rooms that still need dependable automatic temperature control.
A simple Wattz wall vent for equalizing temperatures between adjacent rooms without opening a passageway.
A Poseidon hydro unit designed to draw steady power from moving water. Crowded placements create turbulence and reduce output.
A utility-grade wind turbine for civilian settlements. It needs a broad, clear approach to keep airflow stable.

An ancient generator powered by a reactor core. Once capable of producing massive amounts of power, it has now fallen into disrepair. The containment shield is leaking, causing toxic fallout to blanket the whole region.\n\nHack or destroy the reactor to obtain the core. Be warned, it may explode if damaged.
A control unit that synchronizes the Hellion reactor with its VTOL thrust systems. It reduces the fuel needed for Hellion flight.\n\nOne Hellion can support two fuel regulators.

Prevents the local map from being destroyed during a Hellion launch.\n\nMooring masts stabilize the launch zone around the aircraft and keep the surrounding settlement intact during departure. They cannot be constructed on the Hellion itself, because doing so would prevent takeoff entirely.
A compact pre-war reactor core recovered from prototype Hellion systems and related military hardware. It is useless by itself, but it can be integrated into advanced Hellion support equipment.
A compact generator built around a salvaged reactor core, adapted to provide steady power for the Hellion's onboard systems.

The pre-war reactor assembly at the heart of the Hellion. It sustains the aircraft's vertical lift envelope and powers the heavy systems needed for long-range atmospheric and orbital operations.

When launched, anything on deck plating connected to the reactor will be transported to a new map. Without a mooring mast, launching the Hellion will abandon your current map.

The Hellion was designed as an unfinished military prototype, requiring rare components, specialist fabrication, and a great deal of salvage to keep flying.

A support assembly used to expand the Hellion's airframe. It increases the amount of deck plating the craft can carry and must be built near the Hellion reactor.\n\nOne Hellion can support up to six airframe extenders.

A lightweight pre-war airframe panel used to assemble and reinforce Hellion components. It is durable, heat-resistant, and ideal for advanced aerospace construction.\n\nRequires vacstone blocks, which can be obtained from orbital asteroids.

An airtight armored hull section sized for the Hellion's frame. It seals the craft against vacuum exposure and locks directly into the underlying deck plating, so it can only be built as part of the Hellion.

A defensive projector that protects the Hellion by generating a short-lived repulsor screen against incoming rounds and mortar fire. Those inside the field can still shoot out.\n\nIt must be charged before use. One Hellion can support two shield projectors.

An advanced VTOL thruster with a heat-resistant vacstone liner. It greatly increases the Hellion's operational range. Thrusters require an unobstructed exhaust area free of large buildings and Hellion deck plating.

Requires vacstone blocks, which can be obtained from orbital asteroids.

One Hellion can support up to six heavy VTOL thrusters.
A hardened flight console used to launch and pilot the Hellion. It draws power directly from the Hellion reactor.

A low-grade navigation core tuned for Hellion flight. It reduces the chance of an accident when flying the Hellion, though it cannot fully compensate for pilot error.\n\nOne Hellion can support up to two navigation cores.
A chunk of robot craft made of steel and salvaged airframe panels. It looks like it was torn free during re-entry or blasted off by heavy weapons.\n\nDeconstruct it to obtain valuable resources.
An electronic countermeasures suite that masks the Hellion's signature and helps it approach heavily defended landing sites.

A compact VTOL thruster that increases the Hellion's operational range. Thrusters require an unobstructed exhaust area free of large buildings and Hellion deck plating.\n\nOne Hellion can support up to four small VTOL thrusters.
A small crate containing heavy uranium shells. Used by gauss artillery to reload. A crate is fed directly into adjacent Hellion artillery when it needs to be rearmed.
An auxiliary interface that allows a copilot to assist in launching and piloting a Hellion. It draws power directly from the Hellion reactor.

A compact electromagnetic cannon that launches dense uranium slugs at extreme velocities. Designed for local bombardment and medium-range interception, it can also target hostile orbital craft. Requires a linked targeting terminal with an operator to function. It draws power directly from the Hellion reactor.

A massive reactor assembly that channels a salvaged reactor core into immense electrical output. Its reinforced coils can handle extreme load, making it one of the most potent power sources available for Hellion systems. Adjacent reactor-core power cells multiply the output by 15% each.

A heavy reactor package built for the largest Hellion frames and orbital support hulls. It sustains an enormous lift field, allowing massive aircraft to rise under their own power while operating near a planetary gravity well.

When launched, anything on Hellion deck plating connected to the reactor will be transported to a new map.

These oversized assemblies were intended for pre-war logistics craft and mobile aerospace platforms, and they demand immense fabrication capacity to keep operational.

A compact reactor package adapted for a light Hellion. It generates the lift field needed for rapid vertical launch and short-range atmospheric insertion, but it is not suited to true interplanetary travel.

When launched, anything on Hellion deck plating connected to the reactor will be transported to a new map.

Often salvaged from scout craft, courier platforms, and other pre-war aerospace projects, these reactors are valued for their agility and relatively modest support requirements.
Stores large amounts of electricity for later use. Constructed in Hellion deck plating, Hellion capacitors do not obstruct movement.
A rapid-fire gun for point-defense use aboard the Hellion.
An interface that allows launching and piloting a heavy Hellion. It draws power directly from the heavy Hellion reactor.
An interface that allows launching and piloting a light Hellion. It draws power directly from the Hellion reactor.

A specialized defensive turret built exclusively for the Hellion. It intercepts explosive projectiles and incoming drop pods within its protective radius, but cannot engage ground targets. Interception chance scales with the target's velocity.
Produces electricity from sunlight. Does not work in darkness or under artificial light. It does not obstruct movement or provide cover, but it can only be constructed on Hellion deck plating.
A control station dedicated to operating a single Hellion artillery piece. Requires a crew member to manually aim and fire the weapon. It draws power directly from the Hellion reactor.
A heavy autocannon salvaged from a Vertibot wreck.
The cockpit of a crashed Vertibot, filled with old controls and shattered glass.
Landing gear from a Vertibot, twisted and scorched from the crash.
The remains of a Vertibot, a pre-war military transport aircraft. Scattered with pre-war tech.
A wearable antenna for interfacing with RobCo robots. Allows remote control and communication.
Helmet made out of Eyebot parts. Used for commanding RobCo robots.
Helmet built after design documents of a powerful pre-war hero known as the Mechanist. Grants advanced control over RobCo robots.
An advanced headset for RobCo robot control, featuring improved bandwidth and reliability.
Direct neural interface for RobCo robots, enabling seamless control and feedback.
Armor built after design documents of a powerful pre-war hero known as the Mechanist. Provides superior protection and robot command capabilities.
Harddrive that expands the bandwidth for controlling multiple RobCo robots.
Harddrive containing control protocols for RobCo robots.
Node for managing RobCo robot control bands.
Civilian RobCo robots can recharge here. Produces and stores wastepacks internally.
A holotape for the Pipboy, used to expand control groups for RobCo robots. Insert into the Pipboy to upgrade your robot command capabilities.
A holotape for the Pipboy, used to expand control groups for RobCo robots beyond standard limits. Insert into the Pipboy to unlock high-tier robot command capabilities.
A RobCo device designed to clear vegetation. Emits hazardous energy.
Device for assembling RobCo robots from components.
A pre-war transponder by RobCo Industries.
A RobCo device for assembling and repairing robots.
A RobCo-branded remote energy and computation enhancement device. Any friendly RobCo robots in its effect radius will move and work faster, thanks to proprietary RobCo signal technology.
A RobCo capsule for deploying robots. Contains pre-war robots ready for action.
A RobCo beacon for guiding robot drops.
A holotape for the Pipboy, used to optimize the crafting process for RobCo robots. Insert into the Pipboy to increase robot production speed.
Device for assembling RobCo robots from components.
The Pipboy is a wrist-mounted device used to control RobCo robots. It provides remote access, status monitoring, and direct command capabilities for all RobCo robot units.
The depletet remains of a Sentry Bot fusion cell.
A RobCo device for targeting orbital robot army deployments.
The severed head of a broken assaultron.
A holotape for the Pipboy, used to enable remote repair of RobCo robots. Insert into the Pipboy to activate advanced repair protocols.
A holotape for the Pipboy, used to project remote shields onto RobCo robots. Insert into the Pipboy to enable advanced shielding capabilities.
A holotape for the Pipboy, used to optimize repair speed for RobCo robots. Insert into the Pipboy to increase robot repair efficiency.
Sleek, predatory killer: fast as a shadow, eyes burning with focused death beams. The wasteland's mechanical reaper.
A compact Assaultron-pattern head laser with a faster firing cycle than heavier beam projectors. This makes it more suitable for rapid engagements and close-quarters combat.
This is a Sheepsquatch. Well, not really, but if you squint your eyes it may look like the real one.
Broken Pieces of an Eyebot. A mechanic can study these parts to help unlock higher tiers of RobCo research.
Further boosts the performance of the comms console.
A scavenger drone, endlessly trundling after cans, junk, or "valuable resources." Programmed to hoard like a metallic raccoon.
Comms console with RobCo bossgroup functionality.
Industrial-grade version, all welders and rivet guns. Once meant for building cities of old.
Half hound, half machine, all loyalty. Cybernetics enhance speed, smell, and bite, but the heart remains canine.
A short-range acoustic projector used by RobCo security units. It emits a concussive sonic pulse that disrupts mechanical targets without relying on direct physical damage.
A floating loudspeaker with a mind of its own, tirelessly blaring propaganda and tunes while sweeping the wastes with its mounted energy weapon.
This is a strange Robot, it seems to collect data and send them... somewhere.
A hardened military variant of the Eyebot: thicker armor, heavier gun, and advanced comms gear.
This is one of many eyes of the Hydra.
Rare factory-automation unit, built to oversee entire assembly lines with chilling efficiency. A walking production floor.
This is the Hydra, a dangerous foe of many wasteland legends. They say when you slice off one of its eyes two more grow back.
Integrated laser cannon mounted on a Securitron. Lethal combat weapon.

A propaganda-minded combat robot from Jiang's faction. Humanoid, aggressive, and programmed to view itself as a freedom fighter. Effective in battle despite the existential contradiction of its "liberation" protocol.
Military-grade laser rifle integrated into a Chinese Liberator combat robot.

The ultimate expression of pre-war military technology. A gargantuan humanoid war machine equipped with mini-nuke launchers and plasma cannons. Treats everything it encounters as a potential threat. Extremely rare and almost certainly one-of-a-kind.
A high-energy pulse cannon integrated into Liberty Prime, leaving the mini-nuke throw to its dedicated launch system.
Greatly boosts the performance of the comms console.
The Handy's daintier sister, fitted with programming in etiquette, childcare, and needlework. Less combat, more compassion.
Agricultural Handy variant with crop sensors, pesticide sprayers, and tireless cheer for "the simple country life."
Military-grade Handy,  and merciless, with  and a drill-sergeant personality.
Floating domestic butler, cheerfully armed with buzz saw, flamethrower, and endless commentary. RobCo's most iconic host.
The workhorse of RobCo: boxy, slow, and unnervingly polite. Equally ready to hand out library fines or lethal force.
Boosts the performance of the comms console.
Human intellect entombed in steel. Their eerie, monotone logic is a reminder of what happens when war and science mix too freely.
A precision syringe gun that fires metal needles loaded with toxic compounds. It is designed to injure the target on impact and deliver poison at longer range than most sidearms.
Compact integrated laser mounted on a Scurrybot surveillance unit.
Upgraded laser platform integrated into a Securitron Mark II with an onboard rocket battery.
Integrated ballistic repeater mounted on a Securitron Mark I security platform.
A classic Securitron Mark I security robot. Equipped with an integrated laser cannon for lethal enforcement. Reliable and deadly.
An improved Securitron Mark II with non-lethal electroshock capability. Preferred by law enforcement for control and containment of dangerous individuals.
For homeowners who prefer overwhelming hospitality. Its laser minigun sweeps intruders with steady suppressive beams, keeping the neighborhood impressively quiet.
It's a robot, it will be your death, it's a Deathbot, no more questions.
Your all-purpose household critter remover. It fogs the area with toxic gas and tidies up stubborn pests with a warm, cleansing burst of flame.
A heavy-duty yard guardian for when "keep out" signs just aren't enough. Its breaching rockets turn raider cover, and the occasional garden shed, into decorative rubble.
A high-output head-mounted beam projector built around a long charge cycle and a large blast radius. The discharge produces significant heat and is most effective when fired from standoff range.
A stripped-down Protectron model built for labor and logistics. Cheap, sturdy, and great for mining.
A colossal humanoid war engine. Can throw mini-nuke projectiles and spawn Liberators to aid in combat. Weighs several tons and packs enough firepower to level buildings. A walking WMD.
A sustained pulse battery integrated into the War Machine's torso hardpoints.
The crash site of a Vertibot, possibly containing valuable robotics salvage and pre-war technology.
The crash site of a Vertibot, possibly containing valuable robotics salvage.
Still functional parts of a Mister Handy unit thruster.
Combat RobCo robots can recharge here. Produces and stores wastepacks internally.
This terminal can be used to create RobCo AI routines.
This is a human brain... in a jar.
A General Atomics machine to carefully extract human processor units.
This machine can create AI subroutines by mimicking human brain activity.
A platinum data storage chip which contains a pre-war OS.
A volatile RobCo power cell. Handle with extreme caution!
A barrel for fermenting rot gut.
A harsh, flammable wasteland liquor distilled from chemfuel. Rot gut dulls pain and misery for a while, but it leaves the drinker soaked in volatile fumes and can turn a bad death into a fireball.

A mutated offshoot of ginseng whose roots burrow deep through loose soil until they tap underground oil pockets. Farmers cultivate radseng for the chemfuel-rich sludge in its roots, even though a bad spark can turn the field into an inferno.
A travel-worn gaming table used for caravan games and roadside wagers. Allows up to two players at a time.
A wood-fueled station for brewing wasteland extracts.
An electrically powered station for brewing wasteland extracts.
An extract is causing the user to lose memories quickly, shortening both positive and negative memories far faster than normal.
A patched-up practice target mounted on a crude stand. It is kept around as a placeholder range fixture for anyone still trying to train their aim.
An extract is shutting the user's body down into a deep coma-like state.
An extract is boosting the user's global work speed while slowing energy loss, but it also raises the risk of a heart attack and leaves them with a major sleep-rate crash afterward.
An extract is artificially increasing the user's fertility. The boost is strong enough to give even infertile people a chance to become parents.

An extract is heightening the user's ability to learn, letting them gain knowledge faster, eat faster, rest quicker, and gain more psyfocus while the potent phase lasts. The effects reverse when it fades.
An extract is improving this person's natural wound regeneration, letting them recover from injuries significantly faster than normal.
An extract is improving this person's immune system, making them more capable of fighting off diseases and infections at the cost of making them sleepier.
An extract is providing mental clarity, helping the user focus on skills they are passionate about at the cost of diminished combat capability.
An extract is dulling the user's sense of pain at the cost of greatly reduced focus and productivity.
A hardy wasteland vine that yields dark, bloody fruit when harvested. Its juice is prized for rough trade liquor and survival tonics, and the plant can be harvested repeatedly once established.
An extract is poisoning the user and rapidly degrading their condition.
An extract is hardening the user's skin into a built-in layer of armor that resists burns and physical trauma, though it makes them much less attractive.
An extract is sharpening the user's strength and reflexes, improving combat ability at the cost of higher social fight chance and a higher mental break threshold.
An extract is causing severe pain to the user, leaving them unable to move. Prisoners lose resistance quickly, and slaves become fully suppressed.
A straw-stuffed practice dummy lashed to a post. It is little more than a placeholder training fixture, but still lets people work on their form without risking a real fight.
A blood pump used to hasten recovery from vitamin deficiency.
A serum designed to increase the body's ability to withstand Wendigo sickness.
A containment casket for those suffering from cannibal's vitamin deficiency syndrome known as "The Shakes".
A blood pump used to create muscle mineral reserves.
A compact incubation tank built to mature unstable West Tek specimens from growth cell to full release.
A West Tek growth vat where unstable strain templates are drowned in Forced Evolutionary Virus and coaxed into viable test organisms.
A West Tek extraction rig that renders subject tissue into condensed strain samples for later recombination and vat work.
An advanced West Tek workbench for compounding boosters, stabilizers, and implant-grade augment parts for mutant research.
A stabilized West Tek strain frame used by the FEV vat as the baseline lattice for new experimental growth stock.
A portable containment sac holding an unstable FEV embryo until it can be transferred into a West Tek incubation tank.
A heavy growth tank for larger prototypes and mature mutant stock that would never fit inside a compact incubation chamber.
A brutal West Tek processing bench that tears specimen tissue down into usable strain templates and recovers viable experimental material from corpses.
A blood pump used to increase hemogen absorption.
A blood pump used to help with bloodflow and relax muscles.
A blood pump for stabilizing mental symptoms, relaxing their mind and helping them to focus.
A resonant cryptic stone studied by Whitespring researchers. It increases cryptid meditation focus and is often used in enclave research facilities.
A suit of heavy armor used by Whitespring secret service units to break fortified positions in frontal assaults when no other option is available.

A suit of heavy armor, built for maximum protection at the cost of mobility. Heavy layered plasteel-weave plates with solid ablative coatings stop all but the most well-aimed or powerful attacks. Neuro-memetic assistors prevent the suit's massive weight from immobilizing the wearer entirely, but the suit is still quite cumbersome.\n\nExpensive and beautiful, prestige armor like this is usually made for those of high status. Embedded cryptex threads also enhance the wearer's cryptid sensitivity.

A heavily-armored high-tech helmet, with a solid plasteel-weave shell and indirect visual system for non-frontal view angles. It protects against all but the most powerful or luckily-aimed shots.\n\nExpensive and beautiful, prestige armor like this is usually made for those of high status. Embedded cryptex threads also enhance the wearer's cryptid sensitivity.

A high-tech armored helmet with a built-in status computer and a protective plasteel-weave plate.\n\nExpensive and beautiful, prestige armor like this is usually made for those of high status. Embedded cryptex threads also enhance the wearer's cryptid sensitivity.
A variant of recon armor with short-range thrust capabilities for quick movement in urban environments. Urban scouts prefer close-quarters engagements and quick strikes rather than static defense.
A variant of marine armor specialized for cold and arctic operations, fitted with a shoulder-mounted launcher. Arctic marines specialize in breaching cold-climate fortifications.
A marine armor helmet used by Whitespring patriots, with a built-in status computer and layered plasteel-weave plates. Embedded cryptex threads also enhance the wearer's cryptid sensitivity.

A suit of armor used by Whitespring patriots. Layered plasteel-weave plates are very effective at stopping attacks, with few vulnerable joint sections. Neuro-memetic assistors allow a human to wear the armor and still move easily.\n\nExpensive and ceremonial, patriot marine armor marks membership and rank. Embedded cryptex threads also enhance the wearer's cryptid sensitivity.

A suit of light armor, designed to permit quick movement. Plasteel-weave plates block attacks without compromising mobility, and neuro-memetic assistors in the lower body allow a human to wear the armor and still move easily.\n\nExpensive and beautiful, prestige armor like this is usually made for those of high status. Embedded cryptex threads also enhance the wearer's cryptid sensitivity.
A beret styled with the Whitespring insignia, worn by enclave personnel of certain ranks.
A cap worn by senior Whitespring researchers, denoting a leadership role in cryptid studies.
A tight skullcap designed for cryptid research focus. It provides no physical protection, but its special materials enhance the wearer's cryptid sensitivity.
A lightweight helmet designed for cryptid research focus. While it provides little physical protection, its special materials enhance the wearer's cryptid sensitivity and dissipate neural heat.
A shirt embedded with special cryptid-focusing materials. These enhance cryptid sensitivity and help dissipate neural heat.
A long, formal longcoat used by Whitespring patriots and officers. Embroidered with the enclave sigils, it marks membership and rank.
A stump left behind after an Mothman totem has been felled. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
An ornate grand chair used by the President for official addresses and meditation. Sitting in the presidential grand chair grants the occupant a sense of authority and dignity.

A crystal-metallic longsword refined for Whitespring security officers. Its mono-molecular edge cuts through armor with ease, while an embedded cryptex core sharpens the wielder's cryptid insight and stabilizes the weapon's high-speed response.\n\nThe cryptex core can attune to only one bearer at a time. Once that bond is established, the blade responds with extreme speed and precision and rejects any wielder outside its assigned handler.

A metal-cored officer's sword wrapped in a sheath of captive plasma. Targets are sliced by the inner edge and burned by the surrounding field, giving Whitespring officers a close-range weapon that can cut and cauterize in the same strike.\n\nIts cryptex guidance core can attune to only one bearer at a time. Once linked, the sword responds with unusual speed and control and rejects any wielder outside its assigned handler.

A Whitespring warhammer with an embedded EMP capacitor. On impact it discharges a powerful pulse in addition to the physical blow, making it especially effective against power armor, robots, and other advanced hardware.\n\nIts cryptex guidance core can attune to only one bearer at a time. Once linked, the hammer tracks the wielder's timing and strike rhythm with unusual accuracy and rejects any hand outside its assigned operator.
A large shrine for honoring cryptid forces. Whitespring operatives use these to amplify cryptid focus during ritualized study.
A small shrine used by cryptid researchers to focus meditation and study. These shrines strengthen cryptid-focused meditation for Whitespring operatives.

Mysterious black eggs with a red glow. Tribal peoples find that these eggs grow around the base of a Mothman totem as they meditate. It seems to reflect some kind of strengthening of the totem's cryptic power.

Wasteland scientists have tried to study the phenomenon, but the eggs fail to hatch when observed too closely.

Animals refuse eat Mothman eggs. Tribal stories say this is done out of respect and fear.
A Mothman totem used by Appalachia cultists to focus their meditation and worship.
The remnants of an Mothman totem destroyed by damage. It's ugly. The stump can be extracted but yields very little usable wood. Left outdoors, the stump will deteriorate in time.
An ornate chair used by the President for official addresses and meditation. Sitting in the presidential chair may be used as a focus for certain duties and ceremonies.

Vertibot is a spacer-tech, unmanned aerial vehicle equipped with advanced sensors and a missile payload. Designed for autonomous long-range operations, it's commonly used by the Whitespring Enclave for reconnaissance and bombardment. Its ability to release powerful missile salvos makes it effective against various targets. This vertibot is temporarily disabled due to the recharge cycle.
A large, overcharged vertibot battery. Will release a strong electromagnetic pulse when damaged, potentially disabling nearby defenses.
A large military crate of vertibot munitions. Will explode when damaged, sending armed shells flying in random directions.

A multi-use personal stealthboy cloak device engineered based on experimental Whitespring technology. Allows the user to become completely invisible for a brief duration, after which the device requires a short amount of time to recharge.

A small Whitespring crate containing extremely valuable items. May contain critical intel. Due to the special restrictions, only senior Whitespring officials are capable of opening it.\n\nAny attempt to destroy it will destroy the items as well.

A Brotherhood expeditionary device capable of intercepting Whitespring communication. When activated, it will begin intel scraping, emitting a pulse in regular intervals. Each pulse has a chance of generating a small amount of intel, but it may also increase your visibility and even alert a Whitespring strike force. After 10 pulses, the machine burns out and triggers a fiery explosion.

A dossier containing critical information about the Shattered Whitespring Enclave. In order to protect the information from the Whitespring counterintelligence, the information needs to be stored in a physical form. It can be used to plan high-profile assassination attempts, but it can also be exchanged into standard intel via the contraband system.

A Brotherhood expeditionary declassifier is a single-use, ultra-tech device consisting of a brain-scan apparatus and a long, thick needle. It is primarily used by Whitespring Brotherhood expeditionaries for data extraction. By inserting the needle into the skull of a senior official, memories can be downloaded from their brain, forming valuable Brotherhood expeditionary intel. The amount of intel received is determined by the rank of the target official. The person affected by a Brotherhood Expeditionary Declassifier dies in the process, and when used on neutral visitors, it is treated as an act of war.
A spacer-tech vertibird designed for transit between surface and orbit, or between moons of a planetary system.

A chunk of a Whitespring flagship. It seems to have been torn off by some kind of powerful weapon.\n\nHaving landed not long ago, it still contains useful resources which you can acquire by deconstructing it.
A large fuel tank used to refuel Whitespring vertibirds. Will detonate if damaged beyond a certain point, potentially triggering a chain reaction.

The governmental APC is a formidable armored transport truck, known for its impressive cargo capacity. Its armor is impenetrable, ensuring the safety of its contents throughout transit. Once in motion, the APC’s doors remain sealed until it reaches its predetermined destination. While attempting to loot its cargo is futile due to built-in self-destruct protocols, its robust exterior makes it an ideal shield on the battlefield.

An advanced shielding apparatus, the Whitespring mega high-shield projects a vast momentum repulsor field, allowing shots to exit but preventing any from entering. Specifically calibrated for high angles, it's adept at deflecting incoming mortar rounds, vertibot strikes and even orbital slicer beams. The unit can be temporarily disabled by EMP attacks, either by shocking the projector unit itself, or using EMP munitions on the shield. Draws huge amounts of power.
An underground hydroponics bay used by the Whitespring Enclave to feed its personnel. Fully automated and connected to water mains, these farms require no labor to operate.

A dossier containing information about the Shattered Whitespring Enclave. In order to protect the information from the Whitespring counterintelligence, the information needs to be stored in a physical form. It can be used to obtain prewar tech from the Brotherhood expeditionaries, or to gain access to quests and even assassination missions on Whitespring officials.

Decommissioned Whitespring technology since the invention of psylink neuroformers, psychic amplifier upgrades a psylink in the user's mind. It cannot form a psylink by itself, and can only be used by colonists with already present psylink. The user presses the device over the eyes, where it links to the brain directly and restructures part of it. Afterwards, the device disintegrates into worthless ash.
A small surveillance buoy usually deployed around temporary convoy stops. Linked directly with a patrolling vertibot, once triggered, it will send down a small missile salvo on its location.

A small Whitespring crate containing valuables. May contain intel. Opening it requires bypassing several security protocols and takes a short while.\n\nAny attempt to destroy it will destroy the items as well.
A Whitespring computer interface. Linked to the surveillance console, the display bank shows live footage from all surveillance pillars in the sector.

A large pillar with a series of sensors, cameras and motion detectors. Thanks to the sophisticated face recognition system, surveillance pillar can detect the presence of Brotherhood expeditionaries in radius and will increase the visibility of whichever faction has sent them. Can be disabled via electromagnetic pulse, or by simply cutting off the power grid.

A compact stack of data discs produced exclusively by the Whitespring Enclave for knowledge transcription. They are primarily designed for preserving information. When ignited, they release a dense cloud of blinding smoke and sparks, capable of engulfing a wide area in flames.

The Data Printer is a marvel of technological innovation, designed to capture and preserve the intricate knowledge residing within a scientist’s mind. These advanced machines imprint this invaluable information directly onto data discs. Techprints provide foundational knowledge for researchers, shedding light on innovations from more advanced societies.

This terminal serves as the primary interface for operating the Orbital Cannon batteries. Equipped with sophisticated controls and targeting systems, the Orbital Cannon Control Station ensures precision and coordination when deploying the formidable firepower of the Orbital Cannons. Operators need to be within its radius to control it.

A non-alcoholic, pleasantly light, citrusy, and slightly bitter aperitif that owes its flavors and aromas to sweet and bitter oranges, rhubarb, and Whitespring craftsmanship. Its recipe is guarded by Whitespring hospitality offices, and few on the rim have managed to recreate it. Drinking it induces heavy but painless vomiting, completely clearing the stomach and resetting the palate.\n\nWhitespring officials prize aperitif for letting them indulge in even more of the settlement's finest cuisine.

A high-tech helmet with a solid plasteel-weave shell and multi-spectrum sensors that feed telemetry data to the wearer. While it provides excellent protection, its primary purpose is concealment through an intricate optical camouflage system backed by real-time firing solutions. Armor like this is reserved for the Whitespring Enclave's elite assassins due to limited production.

A high-tech suit of powered armor with layered plasteel-weave plates and multi-spectrum sensors that feed telemetry data to the wearer. While it provides excellent protection, its primary purpose is concealment through an intricate optical camouflage system that also assists the user's movement. Armor like this is reserved for the Whitespring Enclave's elite assassins due to limited production.

An exceedingly complex facemask fitted with advanced technology reserved for senior Whitespring scientists. It is designed for designated researchers so they can advance the Whitespring Enclave's needs without rest.\n\nThe inlaid techprint synchronizes with the wearer's brain while they perform research, effectively doubling research speed while restoring rest and recreation.
A majestic rug with a Whitespring-style pattern, for decorating a floor.
A stoney bas relief of Whitespring imagery for decorating a floor.
An intricate rug with a Whitespring-style pattern, for decorating a floor.
An ornate chair used by the President for official addresses and meditation. Sitting in the presidential desk chair can serve as a focus for ceremonial duties and cryptid meditation.

A spacer-tech Whitespring gunship designed for transit between surface and orbit, or between moons of a planetary system. Equipped with a twin-linked autocannon for ground support, these XVB02s often land in the middle of the battlefield to extract stranded troops.

A command staff issued to Whitespring executive officers during inspections, briefings, and emergency command duty. Its cryptex head sharpens cryptid insight and helps regulate neural strain, while the embedded attunement core rejects any wielder outside the assigned chain of command.
The punga fruit makes me feel relaxed and energized, a rare comfort in the wastes.
I feel heavy and cold all the time. I never thought I'd want a piece of punga fruit so much.

I had a proper hot shower in the bathhouse.

I know this is the wasteland, but eating the same dreary slop every single day still wears a person down.
Between diner staples, caravan finds, and campfire experiments, the menu around here stays surprisingly rich.
The inauguration was like a taste of true grandeur, and a moment of participation in something greater.
The inauguration brought a sense of dignity and honor to the participants.
I had to preside in an awful presidential suite. It's like a deliberate insult from my inferiors.

I presided in an unbelievably impressive presidential suite. The commoners will be amazed at my highness.
I presided in a heavenly presidential suite. It matched my grace and nobility.
The inauguration somehow felt petty and self-indulgent at the same time.
The inauguration felt mechanical and lifeless.

This quest chain will have you track down the shattered remains of a pre-war mobile base crawler, a massive classified fabrication platform built to recover, refit, and manufacture advanced military hardware in the field. If you can reconnect its scattered systems, you may be able to seize its surviving forge lines for yourself.

This quest chain will lead you through sealed military silos and continuity bunkers left behind by a dead pre-war command structure. As you loot their dark corridors and piece together old war plans, you will uncover what happened to the garrison that never stood down and to the officer who still rules the ruins below.

This quest chain will have you explore sealed Vault-Tec blacksites and research vaults where pre-war special projects tried to create improved humans through classified FEV and forced-adaptation programs. Recover their surviving equipment, uncover what became of the dwellers trapped inside, and decide whether those experiments should ever begin again.

This quest chain will have you explore the scattered work sites where Lee Moldaver tried to assemble a prototype fusion power system capable of restoring whole grids. If you recover enough data, parts, and field notes, you may be able to finish her work and build your own reactor network.
Call a one-way Brotherhood expeditionary vertibird for your own use, which will transport colonists, items, and animals up to 70 tiles away.

Trade intel to convince the Brotherhood network to create a distraction, increasing the response time on all currently ongoing operations where the counter is already ticking down. This delays the response by 1 minute. Can be used several times.
Trade intel to convince the Brotherhood network to increase your goodwill with a random faction by 5.
Use intel to convince the Brotherhood network to clear your name and reduce your visibility by 1.
Spend intel to taunt a Whitespring strike force into attacking your colony.

The Whitespring Enclave has initiated an orbital inferno protocol on your location. May you find solace in the cleansing flames. You have 30 days before the Whitespring Enclave moves the required orbital beam satellites into position. Unless you manage to bring your visibility back down to Hidden in time, your colony will be glassed by numerous pillars of fire.

You are operating in secret, and the Whitespring Enclave has no knowledge of your allegiance to the Brotherhood expeditionaries. They will not respond to your attacks on their installations, and they will generally not look for you out in the world.

Your actions have become public knowledge and have hit the Whitespring news stations like a storm. The Whitespring Enclave now considers you a serious threat and has placed a high priority on tracking you down. Security measures across all installations have been greatly increased. Furthermore, the Whitespring Enclave will now send more raiding parties to any settlements suspected of harboring Brotherhood expeditionaries. It will be harder to obtain prewar tech and local support as the Whitespring Enclave's propaganda campaign gains strength.

You are now a household name, and the Whitespring Enclave considers you its number one enemy. It is using all available resources to track you down and eliminate you and your followers. The Whitespring Enclave may also employ scorched-earth tactics, bombing or shelling entire areas suspected of supporting Brotherhood expeditionaries. It is willing to sacrifice its own installations if that is what it takes to eliminate them, and it will respond to any aggression with indiscriminate Vertibot bombardment. Most smuggling operations cease, prewar tech becomes incredibly hard to obtain, and Whitespring intelligence will spread disinformation to turn other factions against you. It is a full-blown war now, and the outcome will determine both your fate and the fate of the Whitespring Enclave. Prepare for a difficult fight. Survival is the only option left.

Rumors about Brotherhood expeditionary activity are starting to circulate among lower-ranking officers and soldiers of the Whitespring Enclave. While the Whitespring Enclave does not yet have concrete evidence of your involvement, it is beginning to take the rumors seriously and has increased its security measures. Expect tighter security on Whitespring installations, along with heavy trooper reaction forces as soon as an installation is alerted to your presence. Whitespring patrols are now prominent and will inspect every caravan they come across.

Word of your activity has started to spread within higher command, but the Whitespring Enclave still sees you as a rare, minor annoyance. You can expect patrols to be dispatched to any Whitespring installation under attack, but you should still have enough time to get in and out before they arrive. Inventory checks become part of the updated briefing, making it slightly harder to acquire prewar tech.

Wastelanders are the backbone of the post-nuclear world. Neither mutants nor vault-born, they are scavengers, traders, raiders, and settlers who made the open scars of the earth their home. Every scar tells a story, every bruise a lesson. They are the living testament that humanity can survive anythingâ€”even itself.

Once human, now cursed by hunger and cold, the Wendigo are vampiric remnants of the old worldâ€™s darkest corners. Their bodies are gaunt, their skin pale as frost, their eyes burning with feral hunger. They feed on blood and flesh not by choice, but by the whisper of something ancient and terrible that will not let them die.

When the bombs fell, the East Coastâ€™s dense cities became tombs of glass and steel. Those who endured the fallout adapted to life in shattered high-rises and flooded metro tunnels. The Atlantic people are resourceful, tech-savvy scavengers who learned to thrive amidst rust, rain, and radiation. They speak with the echo of a thousand pre-war voices, clinging to fragments of the old worldâ€™s ambition.

In the forests of old Cascadia, the sky froze before the earth burned. When nuclear winter blanketed the Northwest, small enclaves learned to live beneath the ash and snow. They are stoic, quiet survivalistsâ€”hunters, herbalists, and scavengers who built warmth from ruin. The cold sharpened them, and they speak in whispers like the wind through pine needles.

On the Colorado Plateau, nature reclaimed what mankind lost. Small, diverse groups survived in hidden canyons, ancient ruins, and red-rock sanctuaries. The Natâ€™L are stewards of the wilderness, descendants of hikers, rangers, and wanderers who refused to abandon the land. They hold a quiet reverence for the world that was and the world that yet survives.

In the ashes of the Southwest, the Rio Grande became a lifeline for those who refused to die divided. Nuevo Texico rose from the union of Americans and Mexicans who shed the old borders. Their blood carries two nations, their speech blends two tongues, and their culture is a hymn to survival, sung in both sun and dust.

Numen are the green children of the wastelandâ€”mutants born where nature refused to die. Vines lace their veins, their skin glows faintly with bioluminescent life, and their breath carries the scent of moss and decay. They are patient, serene, and unsettling, speaking softly of the forests that dream beneath the ruins.

Pure humans are rareâ€”descendants of vault-born bloodlines, preserved genetics, or sheer luck. They see themselves as the last unbroken line of humanity, though in a world reborn through mutation, that purity can be a curse. To some, they are relics; to others, they are hope made flesh.

Slanters are the shadow-folk of the wastesâ€”lean, fast, and pale-skinned from lives spent beneath the surface or under the ruins. Their bodies have grown agile, their eyes wide, their senses sharp. To survive, they learned to vanish: to slip between light and dark, scavenging what others leave behind. Some call them cowards; others call them ghosts.

Super Mutants are the children of the wastelandâ€™s cruel experiments. Towering, musclebound, and nearly immortal, they are the outcome of forced evolution and forgotten ambition. Though often seen as monsters, some retain fractured shards of who they wereâ€”flickers of humanity behind monstrous eyes.

California burned twiceâ€”first in the blast, then in the cold. The survivors of both sidesâ€”American and Bajaâ€”bear the scars of nuclear hellfire and the ice that followed. The Two Californias forged a bitter unity, rebuilding from the bones of cities and the ghosts of surf and sand. They remember paradise, and they remember its end.

Vault Dwellers grew up behind steel doors and sterile lights, sheltered from the chaos above. When the world outside decayed, they evolved withinâ€”disciplined, educated, but naive to the raw savagery of the surface. Some see them as saviors. Others, as pampered relics playing hero in a world that forgot their comfort.
